description:
The actionlib stack provides a standardized interface for
    interfacing with preemptable tasks. Examples of this include moving
    the base to a target location, performing a laser scan and returning
    the resulting point cloud, detecting the handle of a door, etc.

This package provides a set of simple math utilities to work
        with angles. The utilities cover simple things like
        normalizing an angle and conversion between degrees and
        radians. But even if you're trying to calculate things like
        the shortest angular distance between two joint space
        positions of your robot, but the joint motion is constrained
        by joint limits, this package is what you need. The code in
        this package is stable and well tested. There are no plans for
        major changes in the near future.

This package contains a recent version of the Bayesian Filtering
  Library (BFL), distributed by the Orocos Project.  For stability
  reasons, this package is currently locked to revision 31655 (April
  19, 2010), but this revision will be updated on a regular basis to
  the latest available BFL trunk.  This ROS package does not modify
  BFL in any way, it simply provides a convenient way to download and
  compile the library, because BFL is not available from an OS package
  manager.  This ROS package compiles BFL with the Boost library for
  matrix operations and random number generation.
authors: Klaas Gadeyne, Wim Meeussen, Tinne Delaet and many others. See web page for a full contributor list. ROS package maintained by Wim Meeussen.

description:
A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing.  The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

A bond allows two processes, A and B, to know when the other has
    terminated, either cleanly or by crashing. The bond remains
    connected until it is either broken explicitly or until a
    heartbeat times out.

C++ implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when
    another process has terminated.

Python implementation of bond, a mechanism for checking when
    another process has terminated.

The State Machine Compiler (SMC) from http://smc.sourceforge.net/
    converts a language-independent description of a state machine
    into the source code to support that state machine.

    This package contains the libraries that a compiled state machine
    depends on, but it does not contain the compiler itself.
Contains tests for [[bond]], including tests for [[bondpy]] and [[bondcpp]].

description: The class_loader package is a ROS-independent package for loading plugins during runtime and the foundation of the higher level ROS "pluginlib" library. class_loader utilizes the host operating system's runtime loader to open runtime libraries (e.g. .so/.dll files), introspect the library for exported plugin classes, and allows users to instantiate objects of said exported classes without the explicit declaration (i.e. header file) for those classes.

description: The package cob_canopen_motor implements a controller-drive component which is connected to a can-bus and works with a canopen-interface. "CanDriveItf" provides a - more or less - generic interface to the controller-drive components. "CanDrvie..." then implements a specific setup, e.g. an ELMO Harmonica Controller in case of the "CanDriveHarmonica".
The cob_driver stack includes packages that provide access to the Care-O-bot hardware through ROS messages, services and actions. E.g. for mobile base, arm, camera sensors, laser scanners, etc...

The package cob_generic_can provides an interface for nodes on a can-bus and examplary wrappers for two PeakSys-can-libs. When a can-bus-device is generated (for an example see base_dirve_chain) you can use generic_can to create as many itfs as there will be components communicating via this can-bus. Assign type of the can communication device (e.g. usb-to-can or can-card of a specific vendor) and can-address of the target device. This package comes with wrappers for PeakSys and PeakSysUSB adapters.

cob_undercarriage_ctrl implements a controller for the omnidirectional base of Care-O-bot 3 on joint level. For a given Pltf-Twist the according wheel steering angles and linear wheel velocities are calculated based on the principle of rigid body motion. Each joint is than controlled individually to achieve the computed position and velocity

Deprecated
    "cob_utilities" subsumes a number of classes, which are used in the original COb3 software. E.g. "IniFile.h" supports the original inifile structure of Care-O-bot 3. "MathSup.h" provides some basic functions like conversion from degree to radion or norming of angles within +/- PI. The package is currently used while the drivers are ported to ROS and Orocos respectively. Midterm it shall be removed and the ROS structures shall be used for reading parameters during initialization. So, don't use this package in new code!

    can be processed offline using the scripts in this package.

description:
A framework for writing drivers that helps with runtime reconfiguration, diagnostics and self-test.

    This package is deprecated.

The driver_common stack contains classes and tools that are useful
    throughout the driver stacks. It currently contains:

    driver_base: A base class for sensors to provide a consistent state machine
    (retries, error handling, etc.) and interface

    timestamp_tools: Classes to help timestamp hardware events

This package is currently for internal use only. Its API may change
    without warning in the future.  This package is deprecated.

This unary stack contains the dynamic_reconfigure package which provides a means to change
    node parameters at any time without having to restart the node.

eband_local_planner implements a plugin to the
    base_local_planner. It implements the Elastic Band method on the
    SE2 manifold.
authors: Christian Connette, Bhaskara Marthi, Piyush Khandelwal
readme: eband_local_planner eband_local_planner ROS package, implementing plugin base_local_planner move_base navigation system. implements Elastic Band method manifold. support indigo, kinetic distro, melodic testing.

description:
This package provides a set of typedef's that allow
  using Eigen datatypes in STL containers

This metapackage depends on the SMACH library and ROS SMACH integration
    packages.

SMACH is a task-level architecture for rapidly creating complex robot
    behavior. At its core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines. SMACH is a new library that takes advantage of
    very old concepts in order to quickly create robust robot behavior with
    maintainable and modular code.

this package contains a set of messages that are used by the introspection
    interfaces for smach.

The smach_ros package contains extensions for the SMACH library to
    integrate it tightly with ROS.  For example, SMACH-ROS can call
    ROS services, listen to ROS topics, and integrate
    with

The smach viewer is a GUI that shows the state of hierarchical
    SMACH state machines. It can visualize the possible transitions
    between states, as well as the currently active state and the
    values of user data that is passed around between states. The
    smach viewer uses the SMACH debugging interface based on
    the

description: FCL is a collision checking library
readme: FCL Flexible Collision Library Linux Windows Coverage FCL library performing types proximity queries pair geometric models composed triangles. Collision detection: detecting models overlap, optionally, triangles overlap. Distance computation: computing minimum distance pair models, distance closest pair points. Tolerance verification: determining models closer tolerance distance. Continuous collision detection: detecting moving models overlap movement, optionally, time contact. Contact information: collision detection continuous collision detection, contact information (including contact normals contact points) returned optionally. FCL features interface Compilable linux win32 makefiles Microsoft Visual projects generated cmake) special topological constraints adjacency information required input models list model’s triangles Supported object shapes: box sphere ellipsoid capsule cone cylinder convex half-space plane mesh octree (optional, octrees represented octomap library http://octomap.github.com) Installation compiling FCL, Eigen libccd collision checking convex objects https://github.com/danfis/libccd) installed. libccd, compile github version zip file webpage, bug fixing included zipped version. optional libraries installed optional capability FCL. octree collision, install octomap library https://octomap.github.io/. CMakeLists.txt generate makefiles Linux Visual studio projects windows. command line, run mkdir build build cmake linux, compile code. windows, generate visual studio project compile code. Interfaces starting proximity computation, set geometry transform objects involving computation. geometry object represented mesh soup, set set mesh triangles vertice indices std::vector<Vector3f> vertices; std::vector<Triangle> triangles; code set vertices triangles BVHModel template class mesh geometry, default OBBRSS template typedef BVHModel<OBBRSSf> Model; std::shared_ptr<Model> geom std::make_shared<Model>(); add mesh data BVHModel structure geom->beginModel(); geom->addSubModel(vertices, triangles); geom->endModel(); transform object includes rotation translation: rotation matrix translation vector Matrix3f Vector3f code setting transform configured Transform3f pose Transform3f::Identity(); pose.linear() pose.translation() geometry transform, combine obtain collision object instance //geom geometry transform object std::shared_ptr<BVHModel<OBBRSSf>> geom Transform3f //Combine CollisionObjectf* obj CollisionObjectf(geom, objects set, perform proximity computation proximity queries FCL follow common pipeline: set query request data structure run query function request input. result returned query result data structure. collision checking, set CollisionRequest data structure, run collision function: objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* set collision request structure, default setting CollisionRequest request; result returned collision result structure CollisionResult result; perform collision test collide(o1, request, result); setting collision request, user easily choose return contact information slower) return binary collision results faster). distance computation, pipeline objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* set distance request structure, default setting DistanceRequest request; result returned collision result structure DistanceResult result; perform distance test distance(o1, request, result); continuous collision, FCL requires goal transform initial transform included collision object data structure). pipeline distance/collision: objects CollisionObjectf* CollisionObjectf* goal transforms Transform3f tf_goal_o1 Transform3f tf_goal_o2 set continuous collision request structure, default setting ContinuousCollisionRequest request; result returned continuous collision result structure ContinuousCollisionResult result; perform continuous collision test continuousCollide(o1, tf_goal_o1, tf_goal_o2, request, result); FCL supports broadphase collision/distance groups objects avoid square complexity. collision, broadphase algorithm return collision pairs. distance, return pair minimum distance. FCL CollisionManager structure manage objects involving collision distance operations. Initialize collision manager group objects. FCL implementations CollisionManager. Generally, DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManager provide performance. BroadPhaseCollisionManagerf* manager1 DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManagerf(); Initialize collision manager group objects. BroadPhaseCollisionManagerf* manager2 DynamicAABBTreeCollisionManagerf(); add objects collision manager, BroadPhaseCollisionManager::registerObject() function add object std::vector<CollisionObjectf*> objects1 for(std::size_t objects1.size(); manager1->registerObject(objects1[i]); choose BroadPhaseCollisionManager::registerObjects() function add set objects std::vector<CollisionObjectf*> objects2 manager2->registerObjects(objects2); order collect information broadphase, CollisionManager requires settings: callback collision distance; intermediate data store information generated broadphase computation. convenience, FCL default callbacks satisfy call data satisfy collision distance queries. collision DefaultCollisionCallback DefaultCollisionData distance DefaultDistanceCallback DefaultDistanceData. default collision/distance data structs simply containers include request distance structures query type mentioned DefaultCollisionData collision_data; DefaultDistanceData distance_data; Setup managers, related initializing broadphase acceleration structure objects input manager1->setup(); manager2->setup(); Examples queries Collision query object groups collision numbers manager2->collide(manager1, &collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); int n_contact_num collision_data.result.numContacts(); Distance query object groups minimum distance manager2->distance(manager1, &distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); double min_distance distance_data.result.min_distance; collision query group manager1->collide(&collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); distance query group manager1->distance(&distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); Collision query object group entire group manager2->collide(objects1[0], &collision_data, DefaultCollisionFunction); Distance query object group entire group manager2->distance(objects1[0], &distance_data, DefaultDistanceFunction); examples, refer test folder: test_fcl_collision.cpp: provide examples collision test test_fcl_distance.cpp: provide examples distance test test_fcl_broadphase.cpp: provide examples broadphase collision/distance test test_fcl_frontlist.cpp: provide examples frontlist collision acceleration test_fcl_octomap.cpp: provide examples collision/distance computation octomap data data types.

tf is a package that lets the user keep track of multiple coordinate
frames over time. tf maintains the relationship between coordinate
frames in a tree structure buffered in time, and lets the user
transform points, vectors, etc between any two coordinate frames at
any desired point in time.
authors: Tully Foote, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Wim Meeussen

description:
This package contains a set of conversion functions to convert
common tf datatypes (point, vector, pose, etc) into semantically
identical datatypes used by other libraries. The conversion functions
make it easier for users of the transform library (tf) to work with
the datatype of their choice. Currently this package has support for
the Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) and the Eigen matrix
library. This package is stable, and will get integrated into tf in
the next major release cycle (see roadmap).

readme: GSCam ROS package originally developed Brown Robotics Lab broadcasting GStreamer-based video stream standard ROS Camera API. fork fixes incorporated broadcast correct sensor_msgs/Image messages proper frames timestamps. ROS-like configuration control GStreamer interface. Note pacakge built rosbuild catkin workspaces. GStreamer Library Support gscam supports versions GStreamer Default Install dependencies rosdep. Experimental Dependencies: gstreamer1.0-tools libgstreamer1.0-dev libgstreamer-plugins-base1.0-dev libgstreamer-plugins-good1.0-dev Ubuntu Install: sudo add-apt-repository ppa:gstreamer-developers/ppa sudo apt-get install gstreamer1.0-tools libgstreamer1.0-dev libgstreamer-plugins-base1.0-dev libgstreamer-plugins-good1.0-dev sudo apt-get install gstreamer1.0-tools libgstreamer1.0-dev libgstreamer-plugins-base1.0-dev libgstreamer-plugins-good1.0-dev Usage: CMake flag -DGSTREAMER_VERSION_1_x=On building Video4Linux2 launchfile differences GStreamer config lines Notes: tested v4l2src ROS API (stable) gscam run node nodelet. Nodes gscam Topics camera/image_raw camera/camera_info Services camera/set_camera_info Parameters ~camera_name: camera (corrsponding camera info) ~camera_info_url: url (file://path/to/file, package://pkg_name/path/to/file) camera calibration file. ~gscam_config: GStreamer configuration string. ~frame_id: frame ~reopen_on_eof: Re-open stream ends (EOF). ~sync_sink: Synchronize app sink setting false resolve problems sub-par framerates). API (unstable) gscam library guaranteed stable. Examples launchfiles configs examples directory. examples Video4Linux2: Standard video4linux-based cameras USB webcams. GST-1.0: roslaunch argument GST10:=True GStreamer variant Nodelet: Run V4L-based camera nodelet Video File: videofile readable GStreamer DeckLink: BlackMagic DeckLink SDI capture cards (note: requires gst-plugins-bad plugins)

description: hector_gazebo provides packages related to to simulation of robots using gazebo (gazebo plugins, world files etc.)

hector_gazebo_plugins provides gazebo plugins from Team Hector.
     Currently it contains a 6wd differential drive plugin, an IMU sensor plugin,
     an earth magnetic field sensor plugin, a GPS sensor plugin and a
     sonar ranger plugin.
hector_gazebo_thermal_camera provides a gazebo plugin that produces simulated thermal camera images. The plugin uses modified code from the gazebo_ros_camera plugin.
hector_gazebo_worlds provides gazebo scenarios used by Team Hector Darmstadt
hector_sensors_gazebo depends on the necessary plugins for using the sensors from the hector_models repository.

The hector_localization stack is a collection of packages, that provide the full 6DOF pose of a robot or platform.
    It uses various sensor sources, which are fused using an Extended Kalman filter.

    Acceleration and angular rates from an inertial measurement unit (IMU) serve as primary measurements.
    The usage of other sensors is application-dependent. The hector_localization stack currently supports
    GPS, magnetometer, barometric pressure sensors and other external sources that provide a geometry_msgs/PoseWithCovariance
    message via the poseupdate topic.
hector_pose_estimation provides the hector_pose_estimation node and the hector_pose_estimation nodelet.

hector_pose_estimation_core is the core package of the hector_localization stack. It contains the Extended Kalman Filter (EKF)
    that estimates the 6DOF pose of the robot. hector_pose_estimation can be used either as a library, as a nodelet or as a standalone node.

message_to_tf translates pose information from different kind of common_msgs message types to tf. Currently the node supports nav_msgs/Odometry, geometry_msgs/PoseStamped and sensor_msgs/Imu messages as input.
    The resulting transform is divided into three subtransforms with intermediate frames for the footprint and the stabilized base frame (without roll and pitch).
hector_components_description contains URDF xacro macros for robot components, so they are easily attachable to robot models.
hector_models contains (urdf) models of robots, sensors etc.
hector_sensors_description contains URDF xacro macros for sensors, so they are easily attachable to robot models and usable in gazebo.
hector_xacro_tools
hector_compressed_map_transport provides means for transporting compressed map data through the use of image_transport.
hector_geotiff provides a node that can be used to save occupancy grid map, robot trajectory and object of interest data to RoboCup Rescue compliant GeoTiff images.
hector_geotiff_plugins contains plugins that extend geotiff maps generated by hector_geotiff.
hector_imu_attitude_to_tf is a lightweight node that can be used to publish the roll/pitch attitude angles reported via a imu message to tf.
hector_imu_tools provides some tools for processing IMU messages

hector_map_server provides a service for retrieving the map, as well as for raycasting based obstacle queries (finds next obstacle in the map, given start and endpoint
    in any tf coordinate frame).

hector_map_tools contains some functions related to accessing information from OccupancyGridMap maps.
    Currently consists of a single header.

hector_mapping is a SLAM approach that can be used without odometry as well as on platforms that exhibit roll/pitch motion (of the sensor, the platform or both).
    It leverages the high update rate of modern LIDAR systems like the Hokuyo UTM-30LX and provides 2D pose estimates at scan rate of the sensors (40Hz for the UTM-30LX).
    While the system does not provide explicit loop closing ability, it is sufficiently accurate for many real world scenarios. The system has successfully been used on
    Unmanned Ground Robots, Unmanned Surface Vehicles, Handheld Mapping Devices and logged data from quadrotor UAVs.
hector_marker_drawing provides convenience functions for easier publishing of visualization markers.
hector_nav_msgs contains messages and services used in the hector_slam stack.
The hector_slam metapackage that installs hector_mapping and related packages.
hector_slam_launch contains launch files for launching hector_slam with different robot systems/setups/postprocessing scenarios.
hector_trajectory_server keeps track of tf trajectories extracted from tf data and makes this data accessible via a service and topic.
camera_calibration_parsers contains routines for reading and writing camera calibration parameters.

This package provides a C++ interface for camera calibration
     information.  It provides CameraInfo, and handles SetCameraInfo
     service requests, saving and restoring the camera calibration
     data.
Common code for working with images in ROS.

image_transport should always be used to subscribe to and publish images. It provides transparent
     support for transporting images in low-bandwidth compressed formats. Examples (provided by separate
     plugin packages) include JPEG/PNG compression and Theora streaming video.

polled_camera contains a service and C++ helper classes for implementing a polled
     camera driver node and requesting images from it. The package is currently for
     internal use as the API is still under development.

camera_calibration allows easy calibration of monocular or stereo
     cameras using a checkerboard calibration target.

Contains nodelets for processing depth images such as those
     produced by OpenNI camera. Functions include creating disparity
     images and point clouds, as well as registering (reprojecting)
     a depth image into another camera frame.
image_pipeline fills the gap between getting raw images from a camera driver and higher-level vision processing.
Single image rectification and color processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

description:
A simple viewer for ROS image topics. Includes a specialized viewer
  for stereo + disparity images.
Stereo and single image rectification and disparity processing.
authors: Patrick Mihelich, Kurt Konolige, Jeremy Leibs

description:
Compressed_depth_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending
    depth images (raw, floating-point) using PNG compression.

Compressed_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for transparently sending images
    encoded as JPEG or PNG.

A set of plugins for publishing and subscribing to sensor_msgs/Image topics
    in representations other than raw pixel data. For example, for viewing a
    stream of images off-robot, a video codec will give much lower bandwidth
    and latency. For low frame rate tranport of high-definition images, you
    might prefer sending them as JPEG or PNG-compressed form.

Theora_image_transport provides a plugin to image_transport for
    transparently sending an image stream encoded with the Theora codec.

description: ROS-Industrial core stack contains packages and libraries for supporing industrial systems

The Industrial deprecated package contains nodes, launch files, etc... that are slated for 
  deprecation.  This package is the last place something will end up before being deleted.  
  If you are missing a package/node and find it's contents here, then you should consider 
  a replacement.

The industrial message package containes industrial specific messages 
	definitions. This package is part of the ROS-Industrial program.

industrial robot client contains generic clients for connecting 
     to industrial robot controllers with servers that adhere to the
     simple message protocol.
The industrial robot simulator is a stand in for industrial robot driver node(s).  It adheres to the driver specification for industrial robot controllers.
Industrial utils is a library package that captures common funcitonality for the ROS-Industrial distribution.

simple_message defines a simple messaging connection and protocol for communicating 
	with an industrial robot controller.  Additional handler and manager classes are 
	included for handling connection limited systems.  This package is part of the ROS-Industrial 
	program.

ROS driver for a generic Linux joystick.
    The joy package contains joy_node, a node that interfaces a
    generic Linux joystick to ROS. This node publishes a "Joy"
    message, which contains the current state of each one of the
    joystick's buttons and axes.
authors: Morgan Quigley, Brian Gerkey, Kevin Watts, Blaise Gassend

description:
This metapackage depends on packages for interfacing common
    joysticks and human input devices with ROS.

Playstation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3 joystick driver.
    Driver for the Sony PlayStation 3 SIXAXIS or DUAL SHOCK 3
    joysticks. In its current state, this driver is not compatible
    with the use of other Bluetooth HID devices. The driver listens
    for a connection on the HID ports, starts the joystick
    streaming data, and passes the data to the Linux uinput device
    so that it shows up as a normal joystick.
authors: Blaise Gassend, pascal <AT> pabr.org, Melonee Wise

description: ROS interface to the 3Dconnexion SpaceNavigator 6DOF joystick.

The wiimote package allows ROS nodes to communicate with a Nintendo Wiimote
    and its related peripherals, including the Nunchuk, Motion Plus, and
    (experimentally) the Classic. The package implements a ROS node that uses
    Bluetooth to communicate with the Wiimote device, obtaining accelerometer
    and gyro data, the state of LEDs, the IR camera, rumble (vibrator),
    buttons, joystick, and battery state. The node additionally enables ROS
    nodes to control the Wiimote's LEDs and vibration for feedback to the human
    Wiimote operator. LEDs and vibration may be switched on and off, or made to
    operate according to a timed pattern.

description: The respeaker_ros package
readme: respeaker_ros ROS Package Respeaker Mic Array Supported Devices Respeaker Mic Array Preparation Install package Assumed ROS properly installed. install package apt-get. sudo apt-get install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-respeaker-ros build source. mkdir ~/catkin_ws/src ~/catkin_ws/src git clone https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_3rdparty.git ~/catkin_ws source /opt/ros/kinetic/setup.bash rosdep install --from-paths src catkin config --init catkin build respeaker_ros source ~/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash Register respeaker udev rules access USB device permission user space. udev, give permission respeaker device automatically. run command followings install setting file: roscd respeaker_ros sudo $(rospack find respeaker_ros)/config/60-respeaker.rules /etc/udev/rules.d/60-respeaker.rules sudo systemctl restart udev re-connect device. Run generator script. rosrun respeaker_ros respeaker_gencfg.py updated configuration file $(rospack find respeaker_ros)/cfg/Respeaker.cfg. default: Damping threshold dynamic VAD ~dynamic_energy_ratio (Double, default: Energy ratio dynamic VAD ~pause_threshold (Double, default: Seconds non-speaking audio phrase considered complete ~operation_timeout (Double, default: Seconds internal operation API request) starts times ~listen_timeout (Double, default: maximum number seconds wait phrase start giving ~phrase_time_limit (Double, default: maximum number seconds phrase continue stopping returning part phrase processed time limit reached ~phrase_threshold (Double, default: Minimum seconds speaking audio speaking audio phrase ~non_speaking_duration (Double, default: Seconds non-speaking audio sides recording ~duration (Double, default: Seconds waiting speech ~depth (Int, default: Depth audio signal ~n_channel (Int, default: Total number channels audio data mono, stereo) ~sample_rate (Int, default: Sample rate audio signal ~buffer_size (Int, default: Maximum buffer size store audio data speech recognition ~start_signal (String, default: /usr/share/sounds/freedesktop/stereo/bell.ogg) Path sound file bell start audio ~recognized_signal (String, default: /usr/share/sounds/freedesktop/stereo/message.ogg) Explicit implicit. Simple complex. Complex complicated. Flat nested. Sparse dense. Readability counts. Special cases special break rules. practicality beats purity. Errors pass silently. explicitly silenced. face ambiguity, refuse temptation guess. preferably --obvious obvious Dutch. implementation hard explain, bad idea. implementation easy explain, good idea. Namespaces honking great idea Tweet text image tweet images. base64 image path inserted text, jump reply section. Image path wget https://github.com/k-okada.png /tmp/k-okada.png rostopic pub /tweet std_msgs/String "/tmp/k-okada.png" Base64 tweet image encoding base64. python. concatenate multiple base64 images spaces. import rospy import import std_msgs.msg import numpy import matplotlib.cm rostwitter.cv_util import extract_media_from_text rostwitter.cv_util import encode_image_cv2 rospy.init_node('rostwitter_sample') pub rospy.Publisher('/tweet', std_msgs.msg.String, queue_size=1) rospy.sleep(3.0) colormap matplotlib.cm.get_cmap('hsv') text 'Tweet images. description: A simple viewer for ROS image topics with draw-on features
readme: image_view2 image_view2 extended image_view. Features Draw markers image view Interact user direction Interaction Modes image_view2 support mode interact user. set interaction mode ~interaction_mode parameter change mode service interfaces. Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=rectangle) rectangle mode, user select region-of-interest dragging mouse pointer. Selected regions published image/screenrectangle (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. user clicks view dragging pointer, clicked position published image/screenpoint (geometry_msgs/PointStamped) topic. Line mode (~interaction_mode:=line) line mode, user select line dragging mouse pointer. Selected line published image/line (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Polygon mode (~interaction_mode:=poly) polygon mode, user select series closed lines. User add line left-click close lines right-click. Selected polygon published image/poly (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) topic. Grabcut mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut) grabcut mode, user select curves. typing Esc key, clear selected curves. selected red curve published image/foreground (sensor_msgs/Image) green publiehd image/background (sensor_msgs/Image). mode originally implemented select forground background pixels grabcut. Grabcut Rectangle mode (~interaction_mode:=grabcut_rect) grabcut rectangle mode, user select rectangles. typing Esc key, clear selected rectangles. selected red rectangle published image/foreground_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped) green publiehd image/background_rect (geometry_msgs/PolygonStamped). mode originally implemented select forground background regions grabcut. ROS API Publising Topics image/marked (sensor_msgs/Image) Image marks drawed. image/screenrectangle_image (sensor_msgs/Image) Cropped image user selection Rectangle mode. Subscribing Topics image (sensor_msgs/Image) Input image camera_info (sensor_msgs/CameraInfo) CameraInfo image. camera info required compute projection matrix drawing markers. image_marker (image_view2/ImageMarker2) Marker topic. image/event (image_view2/MouseEvent) Event interface. image_view2 handle mouse events gui interaction. topic emulate event handling ros topics Advertising Services ~change_mode (image_view2/ChangeMode) Service interface change interaction mode. ~rectangle_mode (std_srvs/Empty) ~grabcut_mode (std_srvs/Empty) ~grabcut_rect_mode (std_srvs/Empty) ~line_mode (std_srvs/Empty) ~poly_mode (std_srvs/Empty) ~none_mode (std_srvs/Empty) Service interface change interaction mode std_srvs/Empty service. image_view2/ImageMarker2 image_view2/ImageMarker2 message draw image_view2 canvas. readme: jsk_rosbag_tools Tools creating video rosbag compressing rosbag images. bag_to_video.py Create video rosbag. Usage usage: bag_to_video.py [--fps FPS] [--samplerate SAMPLERATE] [--channels CHANNELS] [--audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC] [--image-topic IMAGE_TOPIC [IMAGE_TOPIC input_bagfile rosbag video positional arguments: input_bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output directory path filename. --image-topic interpreted directory file --fps FPS --samplerate SAMPLERATE, SAMPLERATE sampling rate --channels CHANNELS number input channels --audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC --image-topic IMAGE_TOPIC [IMAGE_TOPIC Topic extract. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools bag_to_video.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag --samplerate --channels --audio-topic /audio --image-topic /head_camera/rgb/throttled/image_rect_color/compressed /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.mp4 bag_to_audio.py Create audio file rosbag. Usage usage: bag_to_audio.py [--samplerate SAMPLERATE] [--channels CHANNELS] [--audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC] input_bagfile rosbag audio positional arguments: input_bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output filename. `--audio-topic`_info exists, samplerate channels. --samplerate SAMPLERATE, SAMPLERATE sampling rate --channels CHANNELS number input channels --audio-topic AUDIO_TOPIC rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools bag_to_audio.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag --samplerate --channels --audio-topic /audio /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.wav video_to_bag.py Convert video file bagfile. Usage usage: video_to_bag.py output_file] [--topic-name TOPIC_NAME] [--compress] [--no-progress-bar] inputvideo Convert video bag. positional arguments: inputvideo optional arguments: show message exit output_file, output_file output bag file --topic-name TOPIC_NAME Converted topic --compress Compress Image flag. --no-progress-bar show progress bar. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools video_to_bag.py /tmp/output_bag/head_camera--slash--rgb--slash--throttled--slash--image_rect_color--slash--compressed-with-audio.mp4 /tmp/output_bag/video.bag --compress compress_imgs.py Convert Image messages CompressedImage CompressedDepthImage. Usage usage: compress_imgs.py [--compressed-topics [COMPRESSED_TOPICS [COMPRESSED_TOPICS [--replace] [--no-progress-bar] input_bagfile Convert Image messages CompressedImage CompressedDepthImage positional arguments: input_bagfile input bagfile path optional arguments: show message exit output bagfile path --compressed-topics [COMPRESSED_TOPICS [COMPRESSED_TOPICS image topics compressed --replace --no-progress-bar show progress bar. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools compress_imgs.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag /tmp/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag-compressed.bag tf_static_to_tf.py Convert tf_static save rosbag. usage: tf_static_to_tf.py [--no-progress-bar] input_bagfile Convert tf_static save rosbag positional arguments: input_bagfile input bagfile path optional arguments: show message exit output bagfile path --no-progress-bar show progress bar. rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools tf_static_to_tf.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag Note jsk_topic_tools static_tf_republisher.py republish /tf_static rosbag file. tf_static_to_tf.py approach rewrite rosbag file. merge.py Merges bagfiles. Usage usage: merge.py output_file] [--topics TOPICS] main_bagfile bagfile Merges bagfiles. positional arguments: main_bagfile path bagfile, main bagfile bagfile path bagfile merged main bagfile optional arguments: show message exit output_file, output_file output file --topics TOPICS, TOPICS topics merged main bag reindex bagfile rosrun jsk_rosbag_tools merge.py $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/20220530173950_go_to_kitchen_rosbag.bag $(rospack find jsk_rosbag_tools)/samples/data/2022-05-07-hello-test.bag

The cmd_vel_smoother package
readme: cmd_vel_smoother Interpolate velocity command published periodically acceleration deceleration limit. Basically node interpolates velocity violate acceleration limit decelating received velocity. control loop base_controller cmd_vel seconds, controller stops moving motors safety. move_base node publishes cmd_vel continues publishing, controllers send signal motors stop “rattle” moves suddenly stops repeatedly) damages motors hardwares. Parameters ~x_acc_lim (Double, default: limit x-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~y_acc_lim (Double, default: limit y-axis translational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~yaw_acc_lim (Double, default: limit z-axis rotational acceleration. node publishes velocity violate ~desired_rate (Double, default: node interpolates velocity commands rate ~interpolate_max_frame (Int, default: Max message count published received velocity command. Discussion package originated discussion https://github.com/ros-planning/navigation/issues/405 looked detail implementation similar nodes turtlebot robots exist: https://github.com/yujinrobot/yujin_ocs/tree/devel/yocs_velocity_smoother Author Yuki Furuta «furushchev@jsk.imi.i.u-tokyo.ac.jp»

description: The contact_states_observer package
readme: contact_states_observer Euslisp server check grasping contact states. Grasp state checking grasping state checking, hand joint angle topics. users configure setting :set-grasp-state-param joint angles success-candidates failure-candidates checked joint names. observer program checks difference candidates current hand joint angles find nearest candidate. Finally, observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. Setting parameter (list (list :rarm Success failure candidates (list (list :success (list :fail Checked joint names (list rarm setting (list :larm larm setting list Contact force checking contact force state checking, force sensor thresholding. Observer program checks current force exceeds threshold. Observer program publishes results users obtain :get-contact-states. real robot Start real robot (rtcd ros bridge servoOn) Run program roseus (progn (ros::roseus "test_grasp_check") ROBOT Initialization. HRP2JSKNTS sample (load "package://hrpsys_ros_bridge_tutorials/euslisp/hrp2jsknts-interface.l") (boundp '*robot*) (hrp2jsknts-init) (setq *robot* *hrp2jsknts*)) Loading Contact State Client (load "package://contact_states_observer/euslisp/contact-states-client.l") (setq csc (instance contact-state-client :init)) Set grasp parameter (send csc :set-grasp-state-param (list check larm. returns (list :larm) Set rarm parameter. :success hand's reset-pose, flag :fail hand's hook-pose, flag Check joint angles joints (list :rarm (list (list :success (list :fail (list Test1. Set reset-pose. return '("On" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :reset-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Test2. Set hook-pose. return '("Off" (send *robot* :hand :rarm :hook-pose) (send :hand-angle-vector (send *robot* :hand-angle-vector) (send :hand-wait-interpolation) (unix:usleep Wait hand motion stopping (format '(:rarm :larm) (send csc :get-contact-states '(:rarm :larm))) Publishing Topics ore_contact_states (contact_states_observer/OREContactStates) Array object, robot, environment contact state. contact state consists rleg, rarm,.. state On/Off grasping, foot, hand contact) mode grasp, contact-force, contact_grasp_states (hrpsys_ros_bridge/ContactStatesStamped) Contact states contact point rleg, lleg, rarm, larm) grasp_states (contact_states_observer/GraspState) Grasp state

description: jsk_teleop_joy
readme: jsk_teleop_joy package Introduction package physical teleoperatoin joy sticks, game controllers MIDI instruments. Supported game controllers package supports game controllers: XBox360 controller Controller (Bluetooth) Controller (USB) order controller Bluetooth, instruction. Architecture jsk_teleop_joy jsk_teleop_joy main script joy_main.py) based plugin architecture. ROS packages plugins jsk_teleop_joy plugin represents function pose effector”. enable plugins choose plugin “Pushing Select Button”. jsk_teleop_joy plugins box VerboseStatus JoyPose6D JoyFootstep JoyFootstepPlanner JoyFootstepPlannerDemo JoyMoveIt JoyGoPos implement plugin write xml file define plugins. Export xml file ROS package manifest.xml package.xml. implement plugin Write xml file define plugins jsk_teleop_joy reads plugin definition xml file plugin.xml. xml <library> <class name="Foo" type="your_package.foo"> </class> </library> xml file <library> tag top level. define plugins <class> tag. attribute means plugin. choose plugins launch files type attribute means python class plugin. jsk_teleop_joy instantiate plugin class thie type Export xml file ROS package export xml file <export> tag <jsk_teleop_joy> tag manifest.xml package.xml. files, manifest.xml, package.xml, good examples. Implement plugin Finally, implement jsk_teleop_joy plugin. VerboseStatus Plugin good implement plugin. __init__ method plugins required inherits jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin call JSKJoyPlugin.__init__ __init__ constructor. class VerboseStatus(jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin): def __init__(self): jsk_teleop_joy.joy_plugin.JSKJoyPlugin.__init__(self, 'VerbosePlugin') joyCB method time jsk_teleop_joy receives /joy message, calls joyCB method active plugin. def joyCB(self, status, history): rospy.loginfo('analog left (status.left_analog_x, status.left_analog_y)) argument joyCB instance JoyStatus. JoyStatus XboxStatus, PS3Status PS3WiredStatus. means latest message /joy. classes provide interface don’t care controller user hand, argument joyCB (history), sequence JoyStatus. instance StatusHistory. means hisotry JoyStatus. SELECT button jsk_teleop_joy system occupies SELECT button SELECT button plugin. SELECT button switching plugins. MIDI controllers interactive_midi_config.py configure MIDI devices script interactively. movie Connect MIDI device. script asks device, choose device push buttons/slide faders order sensor_msgs/Joy. script maps buttons sensor_msgs/Joy/axes sensor_msgs/Joy/buttons type quit. configure saved /tmp/midi.yaml. Troubleshooting: Ubuntu18.04 pygame2.0.1 installed pip, python "import pygame.midi; pygame.midi.init()" returns error ALSA lib conf.c:3558:(snd_config_hooks_call) open shared library libasound_module_conf_pulse.so (/usr/lib/alsa-lib/libasound_module_conf_pulse.so: libasound_module_conf_pulse.so: open shared object file: file directory) ALSA lib seq.c:935:(snd_seq_open_noupdate) Unknown SEQ default case, pygame apt pip uninstall pygame apt-get install python-pygame RECOMMENDED: pip installed pygame, symbolic link /usr/lib/x86_64-linux-gnu/alsa-lib /usr/lib/alsa-lib: RECOMMED midi_write.py order control LEDs active faders, output MIDI commands computer. script midi_write.py helps dig command save configuration yaml file option. midi_config_player.py script publishes sensor_msgs/Joy /joy based yaml file configured interactive_midi_config.py midi_write.py. configs directory includes yaml files MIDI devices.

description: Convert audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) to spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image)
readme: audio_to_spectrogram package converts audio data time-series data) spectrum spectrogram data. Usage command, publish audio, spectrum spectrogram topics. set correct args microphone configuration, mic_sampling_rate bitdepth. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram audio_to_spectrogram.launch data conversion pipeline audio_to_spectrum.py spectrum normalized_half_spectrum log_spectrum preprocess node(s) preprocessed spectrum spectrum_to_spectrogram.py spectrogram rosbag audio. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_audio_to_spectrogram.launch Audio Amplitude Spectrum Spectrogram convert data audio spectrum spectrogram data package. rosbag force torque sensor sensing drill vibration. roslaunch audio_to_spectrogram sample_wrench_to_spectrogram.launch Z-axis Force Amplitude Normalized Spectrum Spectrogram Source Spectrum Spectrogram Scripts audio_to_spectrum.py script convert audio spectrum. Publishing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. usual “amplitude spectrum”. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ details. ~normalized_half_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data non-negative frequencies (0Hz-Nyquist frequency)) “normalized” (consistent amplitude original signal). details. https://ryo-iijima.com/fftresult/ https://stackoverflow.com/questions/63211851/why-divide-the-output-of-numpy-fft-by-n https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issue-1550715400 ~log_spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Log-scaled spectrum data. calculated applying log absolute FFT result. log applied “power spectrum”, don’t simplicity. details. https://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_recognition/issues/2761#issuecomment-1445810380 http://makotomurakami.com/blog/2020/05/23/5266/ Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample audio data FFT. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. data_to_spectrum.py Generalized version audio_to_spectrum.py. script convert multiple message types spectrum. Publishing topics audio_to_spectrum.py. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data convert spectrum published. Parameters ~expression_to_get_data (String, default: m.data) Python expression data input message input std_msgs/Float64, m.data. accessing field recommended. complex calculation numpy), transform topic_tools node. ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate input data. ~fft_sampling_period (Double, default: Period sample input data FFT. ~fft_exec_rate (Double, default: Rate execute FFT publish results. ~is_integer (Bool, default: false) input data integer input std_msgs/Float64, false. ~is_signed (Bool, default: true) input data signed input std_msgs/Float64, true. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits input data. input std_msgs/Float64, ~n_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, number channel original matrix. ~target_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, target channel. spectrum_filter.py script filter spectrum. Publishing topics ~output (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Filtered spectrum data (low_cut_freq-high_cut_freq). Subscribing topics ~input (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Original spectrum data. Parameters ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate data generation original spectrum data. ~high_cut_freq (Int, default: Threshold limit maximum frequency output spectrum. ~low_cut_freq (Int, default: Threshold limit minimum frequency output spectrum. spectrum_to_spectrogram.py script convert spectrum spectrogram. Publishing topics ~spectrogram (sensor_msgs/Image) Spectrogram data, concatenation spectrum time series. Image format Subscribing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. Parameters ~image_height (Int, default: Number vertical (frequency axis) pixels output spectrogram. ~image_width (Int, default: Number horizontal (time axis) pixels output spectrogram. ~spectrogram_period (Double, default: Period store spectrum data create spectrogram topic. ~publish_rate (Double, default: image_width spectrogram_period) Publish rate spectrogram topic. audio_amplitude_plot.py script publish audio amplitude plot image. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Audio amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~audio (audio_common_msgs/AudioData) Audio stream data microphone. audio format wave. Parameters ~mic_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate microphone. sampling rate audio topic. ~n_channel (Int, default: Number channel microphone. ~target_channel (Int, default: Target channel. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits audio data. ~maximum_amplitude (Double, default: Maximum range amplitude plot. ~window_size (Double, default: Window size sound input plot. ~rate (Double, default: Publish rate audio amplitude image topic. data_amplitude_plot.py Generalized version audio_amplitude_plot.py. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Data amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~input (AnyMsg) Topic message including data amplitude plot published. Parameters ~expression_to_get_data (String, default: m.data) Python expression data input message input std_msgs/Float64, m.data. accessing field recommended. complex calculation numpy), transform topic_tools node. ~data_sampling_rate (Int, default: Sampling rate input data. ~is_integer (Bool, default: false) input data integer input std_msgs/Float64, false. ~is_signed (Bool, default: true) input data signed input std_msgs/Float64, true. ~bitdepth (Int, default: Number bits input data. input std_msgs/Float64, ~n_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, number channel original matrix. ~target_channel (Int, default: input scalar, input flattened matrix, target channel. ~maximum_amplitude (Double, default: Maximum range amplitude plot. ~window_size (Double, default: Window size input data plot. ~rate (Double, default: Publish rate data amplitude image topic. spectrum_plot.py script publish frequency amplitude plot image. Publishing topics ~output/viz (sensor_msgs/Image) Frequency amplitude plot image. Subscribing topics ~spectrum (jsk_recognition_msgs/Spectrum) Spectrum data calculated audio FFT. Parameters ~min_amp (Double, default: Minimum amplitude plot. ~max_amp (Double, default: Maximum amplitude plot. ~queue_size (Int, default: Queue size spectrum subscriber. ~max_rate (Double, default: Maximum publish rate frequency amplitude plot image. description:
roseus_smach

     * Euslisp state machine class. it will be moved.
     * Message publisher for visualizing current state by smach_viewer.
     * Simple pickle dump script for debugging state machine.
     * Execute state machine as a action server.
readme: roseus_smach package includes euslisp implementation state machine smach. requirements roseus smach smach_viewer Optional visualization. install package manually: sudo apt install ros-$ROS_DISTRO-smach-viewer sample Sample codes sample directory. rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l simple state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-simple) nested state machine rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-nested) state machine userdata rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach state-machine-ros-sample.l (exec-smach-userdata) sample/parallel-state-machine-sample.l state machine parallel action execution rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py rosrun roseus_smach parallel-state-machine-sample.l (demo) Writing Simple Smach(state-machine) codes #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (setq count (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) (defun smach-simple (instance state-machine :init))) (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) (send :add-node (instance state :init :BAR 'func-bar)) goal-states generated method (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) select node start-node (send :start-state :FOO) nodes selected symbol (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) (send :add-transition :FOO :outcome4 :outcome2) (send :add-transition :BAR :FOO :outcome2) (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) Code Explained (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. (defun func-foo (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state FOO~%") (cond count (incf count) :outcome1) :outcome2))) (defun func-bar (userdata-alist) (format "Execute state BAR~%") :outcome2) lines define functions hooked state. Note functions called argument, alist declared user-data arguments, return equal transition action) state. (instance state-machine :init)) line creates state-machine instance binds (send :add-node (instance state :init :FOO 'func-foo)) line creates state add node state machine. create state instance, state hook state function. lisp object state names. case, state :FOO hooked func-foo. (send :goal-state (list :outcome4 :outcome5)) (send :start-state :FOO) lines define goal state(s) start state(s) state machine. create multiple start states list state, define multiple goal state. Goal state(s) returns Note state machine state. goal state considered outcome state machine. (send :add-transition :FOO :BAR :outcome1) line defines transition. arguments :add-transition method OUTCOME. line means “adding transition FOO BAR, FOO node returns outcome1”. (send (smach-simple) :execute nil) line sets execute state machine. order view check progress smach_viewer, function exec-state-machine Writing Nested Smach codes add child state-machine node state-machine. #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") (defun smach-nested ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) state instance include state-machine function (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) node instance args :add-node, :start-state, :add-transition ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) sm-top (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Code Explained #!/usr/bin/env roseus (load "package://roseus_smach/src/state-machine.l") previous line imports state-machine class, state class, transition class. ((sm-top (instance state-machine :init)) (sm-sub (instance state-machine :init))) line creates state-machine instance. sm-sub state machine acting state node) sm-top. easy understand state machine function: state machine called, executed, state machine processing eventually returns goal state return function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init sm-sub)) sm-sub instance hooked node sm-top line. line add state machine state function. (send sm-top :add-node (instance state :init "BAS" 'func-bas)) (send sm-top :goal-state :outcome5) (send sm-top :start-state "BAS") (send sm-top :add-transition "BAS" :outcome3) lines define node, goal-state, start-state, transition sm-top. (send sm-top :add-transition :outcome5 :outcome4) Remember goal state(s) state-machine returns outcome return sm-sub goal state(s). goal state sm-sub :outcome4, return sm-sub :outcome4. line adds transition :outcome5 node returns :outcome4. ((foo-node (instance state :init "FOO" 'func-foo)) (bar-node (instance state :init "BAR" 'func-bar))) (send sm-sub :add-node foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-node bar-node) (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) (send sm-sub :start-state foo-node) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node bar-node :outcome1) (send sm-sub :add-transition foo-node :outcome4 :outcome2) (send sm-sub :add-transition bar-node foo-node :outcome2)) lines define behavior sm-sub detail previous simple state machine Note (send sm-sub :goal-state :outcome4) defines goal state, defines return state machine. (send (smach-nested) :execute nil) Finally, sm-top executed Writing Simple Smach (make-state-machine) make-state-machine function easy-way define simple state machine. requires graph-list, func-map, initial-state, goal-states arguments. simple state machine written (defun smach-simple2 (setq (make-state-machine define graph, list (<from-node> <transition> <to-node>) <transition> corresponds node returns nil. '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) define function map '((:foo 'func-foo) foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2 (:bar 'func-bar)) bar returns :outcome2 initial state '(:foo) goal state '(:outcome4))))) node :foo :bar :outcome4 terminate node. node corresponds 'func-foo 'func-bar functions. function 'func-foo returns :outcome1 times returns :outcome2. function 'func-bar returns :outcome2. (:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) means :foo returns :outcome2, transit :outcome4. (:foo :outcome1 :bar) means :foo returns :outcome1, transit :bar. (:bar :outcome2 :foo) means :bar returns :outcome2, transit :foo. simplify state machine definition, recommend users t/nil return node, users able (:foo :outcome4) graph definition. (defun smach-simple3 (setq (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome4) (:foo :bar) (:bar :foo)) '((:foo '(lambda (&rest args) (cond count (incf count) nil) foo returns nil times returns (:bar '(lambda (&rest args) bar returns '(:foo) '(:outcome4))))) tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple2)))" roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple3)))" check state machine behavior rosrun smach_viewer smach_viewer.py` Writing Nested Smach (make-state-machine) write nested state machine make-state-machie function. add state-machine function maps, added node (defun smach-simple-nested (sm-top sm-sub) (setq sm-sub (make-state-machine '((:foo :outcome2 :outcome4) transitions (:foo :outcome1 :bar) (:bar :outcome2 :foo)) '((:foo 'func-foo) function maps (:bar 'func-bar)) '(:foo) initial '(:outcome4) goal (setq sm-top (make-state-machine '((:bas :outcome3 transitions :outcome4 :outcome5)) `((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps ,sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" '(:bas) initial '(:outcome5) goal sm-top)) roseus write '((:bas 'func-bas) functon maps sm-sub)) set "nestaed state machine" careful add machine. `(backquote) (comma). tested roscd roseus_smach/sample roseus state-machine-ros-sample.l "(progn (setq count 0)(exec-state-machine (smach-simple-nested)))"

description: Random walker app for Kobuki

A controller ensuring the safe operation of Kobuki.

    The SafetyController keeps track of bumper, cliff and wheel drop events. In case of the first two,
    Kobuki is commanded to move back. In the latter case, Kobuki is stopped.
    
    This controller can be enabled/disabled.
    The safety states (bumper pressed etc.) can be reset. WARNING: Dangerous!

description: Provides nodes to assemble point clouds from either LaserScan or PointCloud messages
ROS Libraries for filtering specific kinds of laser scans
Filters a laser scan to remove points that are in the current static map

Assorted filters designed to operate on 2D planar laser scanners,
    which use the sensor_msgs/LaserScan type.

This package contains a class for converting from a 2D laser scan as defined by
    sensor_msgs/LaserScan into a point cloud as defined by sensor_msgs/PointCloud
    or sensor_msgs/PointCloud2. In particular, it contains functionality to account
    for the skew resulting from moving robots or tilting laser scanners.

Meta-package of libraries for processing laser data, including converting laser data
      into 3D representations.

description: USB Video Class driver library
readme: libuvc cross-platform library USB video devices, built atop libusb. enables fine-grained control USB video devices exporting standard USB Video Class (UVC) interface, enabling developers write drivers previously unsupported devices, access UVC devices generic fashion. Building libuvc Prerequisites: libusb CMake installed. build, run shell commands: git clone https://github.com/ktossell/libuvc libuvc mkdir build build cmake sudo install you’re set! change build configuration, edit CMakeCache.txt build directory, CMake GUI desired Developing libuvc documentation libuvc https://int80k.com/libuvc/doc/. Happy hacking!

description:
Defines an API for access point control based on 
    dynamic_reconfigure. Other packages must
    implement the API for various access-point models: 
    for example: hostapd_access_point for hostapd-based control or
    linksys_access_point for Linksys router web interface.

SMACH, which stands for 'state machine', is a task-level
    architecture for rapidly creating complex robot behavior. At its
    core, SMACH is a ROS-independent Python library to build
    hierarchical state machines.  SMACH is a new library that takes
    advantage of very old concepts in order to quickly create robust
    robot behavior with maintainable and modular code.
This package containes numerous examples of how to use SMACH. See the examples directory.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys WRT610Nv2 access point with
    a dd-wrt firmware. Other access points models/dd-wrt versions
    may be compatible as long as the web interface is identical.

A ROS node that controls a hostapd-based access
    point. It is mainly intended for use with a wireless 
    network adapter running in master mode. It implements 
    the dynamic_reconfigure interface defined
    in the [[access_point_control]] package.

This package provides mapping from frequencies to
    IEEE802.11 channels and vice-versa.

A ROS node that controls a Linksys access point with
    a Linksys WRT610n-compatible web interface.
The linux_networking package
sdsdddsdsds

Test suite for the packages that are part of the "WiFi Test Setup" project:
    network_monitor_udp, network_traffic_control, hostapd_access_point, linksys_access_point,
    ddwrt_access_point.

A ROS node that watches a given network interface and publishes
     whether it is both UP and RUNNING (indicating that a cable is
     plugged into it and communication is happening, for instance) or
     not.

Facilities to monitor a network connection by sending UDP packets from
    a client to a server, which bounces them back to the client. The client
    collects statistics on latency and loss. The server is a C standalone utility
    or a ROS node. The client can be a ROS node, a standalone utility or a python class.
A ROS node that allows control of network emulation parameters such as bandwidth, loss and latency for a Linux network interface.

description:
MAVLink communication library.
    This library provide unified connection handling classes
    and URL to connection object mapper.

    This library can be used in standalone programs.
readme: MAVCONN library mavlink connection communication library MAVROS. adopted ROS environment splitting individual package removing dependencies rosconsole. Connection URL Connection defined URL. pass URL MAVConnInterface::open_url() connection object. Supported schemas: Serial: /path/to/serial/device[:baudrate] Serial: serial:///path/to/serial/device[:baudrate][?ids=sysid,compid] UDP: udp://[bind_host[:port]]@[remote_host[:port]][/?ids=sysid,compid] TCP client: tcp://[server_host][:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] TCP server: tcp-l://[bind_port][:port][/?ids=sysid,compid] Note: ids URL overrides ids system_id component_id parameters. Dependencies mavros: Linux host Boost Boost.ASIO Boost.Signals2) console-bridge library compiller support License Licensed terms LGPLv3 GPLv3 GPLv3 headers, LGPL).

readme: moveit_python set pure-python bindings ROS interface MoveIt! based earlier moveit_utils package developed part chess_player package. Overview interfaces MoveGroupInterface move arm move_group action. PlanningSceneInterface add/remove collision attached objects. set colors objects. PickPlaceInterface interface pick place actions. MoveGroupInterface MoveGroupInterface easy import rospy moveit_python import rospy.init_node("moveit_py") MoveGroupInterface("planning_group_name", "base_link") values base_link, planning group called “planning_group_name”. PlanningSceneInterface PlanningSceneInterface easily insert remove objects MoveIt! planning scene. Starting version module newer service-based approach apply planning scene updates, robust publishing messages topics asynchronously. import rospy moveit_python import rospy.init_node("moveit_py") create planning scene interface, provide root link PlanningSceneInterface("base_link") add cube size, base_link frame p.addCube("my_cube", remove cube p.removeCollisionObject("my_cube") reason prefer service, simply set “use_service” false add/remove calls. Calling waitForSync republish messages needed synchronize planning scene script MoveIt. p.addCube("my_cube", use_service=False) p.addCube("my_other_cube", use_service=False) p.waitForSync() PickPlaceInterface import rospy moveit_python import moveit_msgs.msg import Grasp, PlaceLocation rospy.init_node("moveit_py") provide arm group gripper group names takes parameter "plan_only" defaults False PickPlaceInterface("arm", "gripper") Grasp() fill setup object named object_name PlanningSceneInterface p.pickup("object_name", support_name "supporting_surface") PlaceLocation() fill p.place("object_name" goal_is_eef True, support_name "supporting_surface") Migration moveit_utils GraspingInterface renamed PickPlaceInterface pick/place functions return entire action result, moveit_utils returned error_code. access error code returned, result.error_code.val ObjectManager renamed PlanningSceneInterface remove function removeCollisionObject removeAttachedObject ArmInterface renamed MoveGroupInterface

description:
The configuration node loads a given launch configuration and offers services to 
     list or start the contained nodes. It provides additional description 
     extracted from launch file. This is used by node_manager_fkie.

Discover the running ROS Masters in local network. The 
     discovering is done by sending an echo heartbeat messages to a defined 
     multicast group.
     The alternative is to use a zeroconf/avahi daemon to register the ROS 
     master as service and discover other ROS masters.

Synchronize the local ROS master to the remote masters 
     discovered by master_discovery_fkie node. The registration
     of topics and services is only perform by local ROS master.

The metapackage to combine the nodes required to establish and manage a multimaster network. 
    This requires no or minimal configuration. The changes are automatically detected and synchronized.
The messages required by multimaster packages.

Graphical interface, written in PySide, to manage the running and 
     configured ROS nodes on different hosts. For discovering 
     the running ROS master master_discovery node will be used.

Given a plan to follow and a costmap, the controller produces velocity commands to send to a mobile base. This package supports both holonomic and non-holonomic robots, any robot footprint that can be represented as a convex polygon or circle, and exposes its configuration as ROS parameters that can be set in a launch file. description: This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by reverting the costmaps used by the navigation stack to the static map outside of a given area.

This package provides an implementation of a 2D costmap that takes in sensor
        data from the world, builds a 2D or 3D occupancy grid of the data (depending
        on whether a voxel based implementation is used), and inflates costs in a
        2D costmap based on the occupancy grid and a user specified inflation radius.
        This package also provides support for map_server based initialization of a
        costmap, rolling window based costmaps, and parameter based subscription to
        and configuration of sensor topics.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, David V. Lu!!, Dave Hershberger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

This package provides common interfaces for navigation specific robot actions. Currently, this package provides the BaseGlobalPlanner, BaseLocalPlanner, and RecoveryBehavior interfaces, which can be used to build actions that can easily swap their planner, local controller, or recovery behavior for new versions adhering to the same interface.

navfn provides a fast interpolated navigation function that can be used to create plans for
        a mobile base. The planner assumes a circular robot and operates on a costmap to find a
        minimum cost plan from a start point to an end point in a grid. The navigation function is
        computed with Dijkstra's algorithm, but support for an A* heuristic may also be added in the
        near future. navfn also provides a ROS wrapper for the navfn planner that adheres to the
        nav_core::BaseGlobalPlanner interface specified in
authors: Kurt Konolige, Eitan Marder-Eppstein, contradict <AT> gmail.com

description:
A 2D navigation stack that takes in information from odometry, sensor
        streams, and a goal pose and outputs safe velocity commands that are sent

readme: ROS Navigation Stack navigation stack takes information odometry, sensor streams, goal pose outputs safe velocity commands mobile base. Related stacks: http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_tutorials http://github.com/ros-planning/navigation_experimental

description: The Robot Pose EKF package is used to estimate the 3D pose of a robot, based on (partial) pose measurements coming from different sources. It uses an extended Kalman filter with a 6D model (3D position and 3D orientation) to combine measurements from wheel odometry, IMU sensor and visual odometry. The basic idea is to offer loosely coupled integration with different sensors, where sensor signals are received as ROS messages.
This package provides a recovery behavior for the navigation stack that attempts to clear space by performing a 360 degree rotation of the robot.

voxel_grid provides an implementation of an efficient 3D voxel grid. The occupancy grid can support 3 different representations for the state of a cell: marked, free, or unknown. Due to the underlying implementation relying on bitwise and and or integer operations, the voxel grid only supports 16 different levels per voxel column. However, this limitation yields raytracing and cell marking performance in the grid comparable to standard 2D structures making it quite fast compared to most 3D structures.
authors: Eitan Marder-Eppstein, Eric Berger, contradict <AT> gmail.com

This package provides a node for higher level navigation of a mobile
    robot in a planar environment. It needs a map and the robot's position
    within this map to create a plan for navigation. When used together with
    a SLAM module it can also be used to perform autonomous exploration of
    the robot's workspace.

The operator is a lightweight, purely reactive obstacle-avoidance
    module for mobile robots moving in a planar environment. The operator node
    works by evaluating a set of predefined motion primitives based on a local
    costmap and a desired direction. The best evaluated motion command will be
    send to the mobile base.

This package is used to manually control a robot that uses the operator and
    navigator node from navigation_2d. Currently there is one node to control one
    robot with a joystick and one to control multiple robots in simulation.
    It can send commands directly to the operator or start and stop navigator actions.

Users will send a goal to an Action in the message format of PR2Gripper*Command (where * replaces the name of the particular Action from pr2_gripper_sensor_action). Feedback and Result information for the action is then returned in the format of PR2Gripper*Data.

description:
This package contains a set of tools that can be used from a hard
    realtime thread, without breaking the realtime behavior.  The
    tools currently only provides the realtime publisher, which makes
    it possible to publish messages to a ROS topic from a realtime
    thread. We plan to add a basic implementation of a realtime
    buffer, to make it possible to get data from a (non-realtime)
    topic callback into the realtime loop. Once the lockfree buffer is
    created, the realtime publisher will start using it, which will
    result in major API changes for the realtime publisher (removal of
    all lock methods).

This package provides an interface for operating the schunk dexterous hand (SDH), including the tactile sensors.

This package provides simulated tactile sensors for the Schunk Dextrous
     Hand (SDH) which is mounted on the Care-O-bot arm. The node subscribes to
     the Gazebo bumper topics of the SDH. It transforms the Gazebo feedback to
     the "tactile_data" topic to provide the same tactile sensor interface as
     the schunk_sdh package.
     
     The following parameters can be set:
     * cells_x: The number of patches on the tactile sensor in the direction
                perpendicular to the finger. Defaults to 6.
     * cells_y: The number of patches on the tactile sensor along the direction
                of the finger. Defaults to 14.
     * output_range: The maximum output value of one patch. Defaults to 3500.
     * sensitivity: The change of output in one patch per Newton. Defaults to
                    350. The sensitivity can be approximated by the following
                    formula: S = output_range / (measurement_range * cell_area)
                    - The measurement range of the tactile pads is 250 kPa (from
                      the data sheet).
                    - The output range can be determined by experiment from the
                      real SDH. It is about 3500.
                    - The cell area is the size of one patch. Length and width
                      of the area are determined by dividing the length/width
                      of the collision surface by the number of cells in the
                      respective direction.
                      Important: In most cases this is NOT the cell area that is
                                 given in the data sheet!
     * filter_length: The length of the moving average filter which smoothes
                      the values from simulation. Defaults to 10.
     
     The node subscribes to the following topics to receive data from the
     simulation:
     * thumb_2/state
     * thumb_3/state
     * finger_12/state
     * finger_13/state
     * finger_22/state
     * finger_23/state
     
     The node publishes the processed data on the following topic:
     * tactile_data
     
     The simulated bumper must obtain the collision data in the link that the
     sensor is attached to. This is achieved by setting the "frameName" property
     in the gazebo_ros_bumper controller.

Virtual package providing ViSP related packages.

Online automated pattern-based object tracker relying on visual servoing.

    visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers provided by ViSP visual
    servoing library into a ROS package. The tracked object should have a
    QRcode of Flash code pattern. Based on the pattern, the object is
    automaticaly detected. The detection allows then to initialise the
    model-based trackers. When lost of tracking achieves a new detection
    is performed that will be used to re-initialize the tracker.

    This computer vision algorithm computes the pose (i.e. position and
    orientation) of an object in an image. It is fast enough to allow
    object online tracking using a camera.
readme: visp_auto_tracker visp_auto_tracker wraps model-based trackers ViSP visual servoing library ROS package. tracked object QRcode Flash code pattern. Based pattern, object automaticaly detected. detection initialise model-based trackers. lost tracking achieves detection performed re-initialize tracker. computer vision algorithm computes pose position orientation) object image. fast object online tracking camera. package composed node called ‘visp_auto_tracker’. node detect QRcode Flash code object. detection performed, node tracks object. lost tracking occurs node detect object restart tracking. viewer comming visp_tracker package monitor tracking result. Project webpage ros.org: tutorial API reference Project webpage: source code download, bug report Setup package submodules. compiled ROS package catkin_make. Prerequisities visp_auto_tracker depends visp_bridge visp_tracker packages https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp (hydro-devel branches). visp_auto_tracker depends libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev system dependencies. install run: sudo apt-get install libdmtx-dev libzbar-dev build visp_tracker Supposed catkin work space run: ~/catkin_ws/src git clone hydro-devel https://github.com/lagadic/vision_visp.git catkin_make --pkg visp_auto_tracker Documentation documentation project ROS homepage. information, refer ROS tutorial.

description:
A multiplexer for command velocity inputs. Arbitrates incoming cmd_vel messages from several topics,
     allowing one topic at a time to command the robot, based on priorities. It also deallocates current
     allowed topic if no messages are received after a configured timeout. All topics, together with their
     priority and timeout are configured through a YAML file, that can be reload at runtime.

    to put common code that is typically repeated in many robot control programs.
