description:
This package contains a C++ parser for the Collada robot
    description format. The parser reads a Collada XML robot
    description, and creates a C++ URDF model. Although it is possible
    to directly use this parser when working with Collada robot
    descriptions, the preferred user API is found in the urdf package.

This package contains a tool to convert Unified Robot Description Format (URDF) documents into COLLAborative Design Activity (COLLADA) documents.

    Implements robot-specific COLLADA extensions as defined by
    http://openrave.programmingvision.com/index.php/Started:COLLADA
This package contains a tool for setting and publishing joint state values for a given URDF.

The Kinematics and Dynamics Library (KDL) defines a tree structure
   to represent the kinematic and dynamic parameters of a robot
   mechanism.

This package retrieves data from url-format files such as http://,
   ftp://, package:// file://, etc., and loads the data into memory.
   The package:// url for ros packages is translated into a local
   file:// url.  The resourse retriever was initially designed to load
   mesh files into memory, but it can be used for any type of
   data. The resource retriever is based on the the libcurl library.

This package contains a C++ parser for the Unified Robot Description
    Format (URDF), which is an XML format for representing a robot model. 
    The code API of the parser has been through our review process and will remain
    backwards compatible in future releases.
This package contains a C++ base class for URDF parsers.

readme: RTT ROS Communications Nomenclature Warning: “ROS Service” remote procedure call hapens ROS communication protocols “Orocos/RTT Service” grouping properties, functions, data ports. “ROS Services” satisfy similar role “Orocos/RTT Operations”. Contents package serves purposes. Orocos RTT Service publishing subscribing ROS topics Orocos RTT Services calling serving ROS services Orocos typekits ROS message primitive types template generating wrapper packages ROS .msg .srv files typekits .msg files transport plugin .msg files ros service proxy factories .srv files Plugins ROS Topics package global RTT service creating real-time-safe connections ROS topics Orocos RTT data ports. package Orocos connection policies: buffered unbuffered connections ROS topics. Publishing subscribing command, topic type inferred Orocos port type. Connection policies created operations: rostopic.connection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates connection buffer length rostopic.bufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME, BUFFER_LENGTH): Creates connection user-supplied buffer length. rostopic.unbufferedConnection(TOPIC_NAME): Creates unbuffered connection, writing thread immediately publishs message (publishing real-time safe. Note TOPIC_NAME prefixed tilde resolved process’s private namespace, similarly topic names resolved rospy. ROS Services package global RTT service task-scoped service facilitating communication ROS services. global service, rosservice_registry register factories creating proxies ROS service clients servers. task-scoped service ued bind RTT operations operation callers ROS services. general, users task-scoped RTT service, similarly rtt_rosparam service task-scoped RTT service rosservice operations: rosservice.connect(RTT_OPERATION_NAME, ROS_SERVICE_NAME, ROS_SERVICE_TYPE) Connect RTT operation ROS service. Note function RTT operation operation operation caller. RTT_OPERATION_NAME: task-scoped operation/operation caller provided/required services separated dots foo.bar.baz.op) ROS_SERVICE_NAME: service client/server ROS graph /some/ros/ns/my_service) ROS_SERVICE_TYPE: full typename service std_srvs/Empty) global RTT service rosservice_registry operations: rosservice_registry.registerServiceFactory(FACTORY): Register ROS service factory rosservice_registry.hasServiceFactory(TYPENAME): Check ROS service type registered rosservice_registry.geServiceFactory(TYPENAME): ROS service client/server factory Code Generation package facilities generating typekits ROS service types defined .srv files generating plugins register ROS service types rosservice_registry service. Usage Connecting Orocos Port ROS Topic Imports import("rtt_roscomm") Publish stream("my_component.my_output", ros.comm.topic("my_ros_output")) Subscribe stream("my_component.my_input", ros.comm.topic("my_ros_input")) set connections code: #include <rtt_roscomm/rtt_rostopic.h> Add port stream ROS topic this->ports()->addPort("my_port", my_port_); my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("my_ros_topic")); create privately-scoped component-scoped topic, Privately-scoped (resolves NODE_NAME/TOPIC_NAME) my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("~my_private_ros_topic")); Component-scoped (resolves NODE_NAME/COMPONENT_NAME/TOPIC_NAME) my_port_.createStream(rtt_roscomm::topic("~" this->getName() "/my_component_scoped_ros_topic")); Connecting RTT Operations ROS Services connect Orocos operation ROS service .ops script Orocos DeploymentComponent: Imports import("rtt_roscomm") import("rtt_std_srvs") Load application-specific component loadComponent("some_component_name","some_component_package::SomeComponent") Load rosservice RTT service components loadService("some_component_name","rosservice") Expose operation component ROS service some_component_name.rosservice.connect( "some_provided_service.some_operation", "/some/ros/namespace/empty", "std_srvs/Empty") Expose ROS service component some_component_name.rosservice.connect( "some_Required_service.some_operation_caller", "/some/ros/namespace/empty", "std_srvs/Empty") Making Package’s ROS .msg .srv Types Generally, create catkin package simply create_rtt_msgs script running: rosrun rtt_roscomm create_rtt_msgs my_msgs create package CMakeLists.txt package.xml: project(rtt_my_msgs) find_package(catkin REQUIRED COMPONENTS rtt_roscomm) Generate typekits ros .msg files ros_generate_rtt_typekit(my_msgs) Generate plugin services my_msgs ros_generate_rtt_service_proxies(my_msgs) Call orocos_generate_package() export proper targets orocos_generate_package( DEPENDS my_msgs DEPENDS_TARGETS rtt_roscomm ros_generate_rtt_service_proxies() cmake function generate RTT plugin registers factories services named package plugin loaded. description: This package provides an RTT service and service-requester for associating RTT component properties with ROS parameters
readme: RTT ROSParam package RTT service service-requester associating RTT component properties ROS parameters. Design RTT service enables setting/getting individual RTT properties RTT component to/from ROS parameter server, setting/getting properties component simultaneously. operations map operations roscpp library, store retrieved values RTT properties component. service requires RTT properties component match names ROS parameters, modulo namespace resolution policy Usage Service Interface Constants resolution policies): RELATIVE Relative resolution: ABSOLUTE Absolute resolution: PRIVATE Private resolution: COMPONENT Component resolution: “~COMPONENT_NAME/name” Operations (getting/setting params) Operations properties getAll() getAllComponentPrivate() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server COMPONENT namespace. getAllRelative() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server relative namespace. getAllAbsolute() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server absolute namespace. getAllPrivate() Attempt properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server node’s private namespace. Operations setting properties setAll() setAllComponentPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property COMPONENT’s private namespace. setAllRelative() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property relative namespace. setAllAbsolute() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property absolute namespace. setAllPrivate() Stores properties component sub-services) ROS parameter server similarly-named property node’s private namespace. Operations single properties getParam(ros_name, rtt_name) ROS param ros_name store RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. get(name ,policy) Attempt property named populates properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. getRelative(name) getAbsolute(name) getPrivate(name) getComponentPrivate(name) Operations setting single properties setParam(ros_name, rtt_name) Set ROS param ros_name RTT property rtt_name. leaders private absolute resolution. set(name, policy) Attempt set property named stores properties named RTT sub-service) ROS parameter namespace policy. setRelative(name) setAbsolute(name) setPrivate(name) setComponentPrivate(name) Scripting Interface Components meant ROS parameters properties set ROS parameter server scripting interface: Import packages import("rtt_ros") ros.import("rtt_rosparam") Create components laodService("my_component","rosparam") parameters component namespace "~my_component/..." my_component.rosparam.getAll() "robot_description" property absolute namespace "/robot_description" my_component.rosparam.getAbsolute("robot_description") Alternatively: my_component.rosparam.get("robot_description",my_component.rosparam.ABSOLUTE) Interface component designed ROS parameters, easily access rosparam service RTT ServiceRequester. simple component “robot_description” ROS param global namespace (“/robot_description”) “publish_period” ROS param node’s private namespace (“~publish_period”) Include service requester interface #include <rtt_rosparam/rosparam.h> class MyComponent public RTT::TaskContext private: Storage RTT properties std::string robot_description_; double publish_period_; public: MyComponent::MyComponent(std::string RTT::TaskContext(name) Add properties this->addProperty("robot_description",robot_description_); this->addProperty("publish_period",publish_period_); bool configureHook() bool all_params_found true; rosparam service requester boost::shared_ptr<rtt_rosparam::ROSParam> rosparam this->getProvider<rtt_rosparam::ROSParam>("rosparam"); parameters if(rosparam) ROS parameter "/robot_description" all_params_found rosparam->getAbsolute("robot_description"); ROS parameter "~publish_period" all_params_found rosparam->getPrivate("publish_priod"); return all_params_found; API documentation information.

description: This package contains the components of the rtt_tf package

Usage general, process single component. component operations bare interface RTT service. “lookupTransform” Lookup transform source target. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “lookupTransformAtTime” Lookup transform source target specific time. “target”, Target frame “source”, Source frame “common_time”, ros::Time common time transform computed “broadcastTransform” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transform”, geometry\_msgs::TransformStamped “broadcastTransforms” Broadcast stamped transform immediately. “transforms”, std::vector<geometry_msgs::TransformStamped> operations, instantiate rtt_tf::RTT_TF component, connect components intend import("rtt_ros"); Import load component ros.import("rtt_tf"); loadComponent("tf","rtt_tf::RTT_TF"); Connect operations component's service component's required service (easily created API shown section) loadComponent("my_component","MyComponent"); connectServices("my_component","tf"); API rtt_tf/tf_interface.h header includes rtt_tf::TFInterface class adds required service RTT component. easily connecting component. create RTT service requester #include <rtt_tf/tf_interface.h> class MyComponent public RTT::TaskContext private: rtt_tf::TFInterface public: MyComponent(std::string RTT::TaskContext(name), Add services tf_(this) bool startHook() ready connected return tf_.ready(); void updateHook() Lookup transform geometry_msgs::TransformStamped tform tf_.lookupTransform("/world","/my_frame");

description: driver for the KUKA youBot robot
readme: youBot API API documentation basic idea youBot API represent robot system combination decoupled functional sub-systems. API represents manipulator arm base platform combination joints. time joint defined combination motor gearbox. main classes youBot API user aware YouBotManipulator class represents youBot arm aggregation joints gripper YouBotBase class represents youBot base platform YouBotJoint class represents joints manipulator base Installation System requirements: Linux Ethernet adapter Root access Ethernet adapter libraries required youBot API: Simple Open EtherCAT master (included driver) Boost Libraries Installation rosmake: Install minimal installation ROS. ros.org) Clone youBot API sources: git clone git://github.com/youbot/youbot_driver.git Add repository folder ROS_PACKAGE_PATH environment variable. Compile youbot driver typing: rosmake youbot_driver --rosdep-install Manual installation: gnu-make software system (version required), working compiler chain. console commands exemplary Ubuntu Linux. git version control software: sudo apt-get install git git-core Cmake cross platform sudo apt-get install cmake download youBot API sources type: git clone git://github.com/youbot/youbot_driver.git Install boost. sudo apt-get install libboost-all-dev able compile youBot API. <your_folder>/youbot_driver mkdir build build cmake library generated folder ~/youbot_driver/lib. Usage youBot API give complete joint level access youBot joints. youBot joint represented youbot::YouBotJoint class API. stage difference base joint powers wheel manipulator joint. classes youbot::YouBotBase youbot::YouBotManipulator access youbot::YouBotJoint instance joint. set setpoint read sensor values form joints youbot::JointData classes. instance youbot::JointVelocitySetpoint youbot::JointSensedCurrent. configure parameters joint, youbot::JointParameter classes. instance youbot::MaximumPositioningVelocity. Programs find programs youbot_applications repository. Documentation detailed information class methods refer API documentation. generate documentation source code type: sudo apt-get install doxygen build doc Run sudo youBot Driver access raw ethernet device. description: The adhoc_communication package allows to exchange data over an ad-hoc network setup by robots running the corresponding node. The package allows you to exchange data over serveral roscores by wrapping transmitting the data to the destination and publishing the data on a predefined topic at the destination host. Routing is accomplished using the hostname of the robots.

The explorer package utilizes frontier based exploration for multi-robot systems. Beside frontier detection, coordinated and uncoordinated exploration strategies are available to select goal points. Coordinated exploration enhances robot distribution and reduces redundancy in exploration reducing exploration time.

description:
This ROS package contains the clasp answer set solver for
    (extended) normal logic programs developed at the University of
    Potsdam.

    Clasp combines the high-level modeling capacities of answer set
    programming (ASP) with state-of-the-art techniques from the area
    of Boolean constraint solving. The primary clasp algorithm relies
    on conflict-driven nogood learning, a technique that proved very
    successful for satisfiability checking (SAT). Unlike other
    learning ASP solvers, clasp does not rely on legacy software, such
    as a SAT solver or any other existing ASP solver. Rather, clasp
    has been genuinely developed for answer set solving based on
    conflict-driven nogood learning. clasp can be applied as an ASP
    solver (on SMODELS format, as output by Gringo), as a SAT solver
    (on a simplified version of DIMACS/CNF format), or as a PB solver
    (on OPB format).
readme: clasp-3.x conflict-driven nogood learning answer set solver http://www.cs.uni-potsdam.de/clasp/ http://potassco.sourceforge.net/ OVERVIEW clasp answer set solver (extended) normal disjunctive logic programs. combines high-level modeling capacities answer set programming state-of-the-art techniques area Boolean constraint solving. primary clasp algorithm relies conflict-driven nogood learning, technique proved successful satisfiability checking (SAT). Starting version clasp supports parallel (multithreaded) solving. Starting version clasp supports disjunctive logic programs claspD-2 domain heuristic modifications hclasp option "--heuristic=domain" unsatisfiable-core based optimization unclasp "--opt-strategy={4,5}" clasp written Standard-C++. successfully built run Linux gcc/clang Windows Microsoft Visual Studio MinGW. Detailed information (including User's manual), source code, pre-compiled binaries http://potassco.sourceforge.net/ LICENSE clasp part Potassco project hosted SourceForge. distributed GNU Public License. COPYING details license. PACKAGE CONTENTS COPYING GNU Public License Major versions README file configure.{sh,bat} Simple script creates Makefiles building clasp (library application) app/ Source code directory command-line interface libclasp/ Directory clasp (static) library (sources, documentation, unit tests) libprogram_opts/ Library parsing command-line options (needed app) build_vc/ Directory Visual Studio project files building clasp tools/ additional files BUILDING INSTALLING preferred build clasp configure script. GNU Compiler Collection (GCC) version installed order build clasp. GNU Microsoft Windows, recommend MinGW http://www.mingw.org/ visit http://www.mingw.org/wiki/Getting_Started detailed instructions installing MinGW. install MinGW-make. assumed 'make' alias installed GNU case system, replace 'make' GNU executable gmake). Microsoft Windows ./configure.bat ./configure.sh. clasp's multithread support requires Intel Threading Building Blocks library (version freely http://threadingbuildingblocks.org/ downloading installing set export TBB30_INSTALL_DIR environment variable. Type ./configure.sh overview supported build configurations/options. build clasp: ./configure.sh build/release build clasp multithread support TBB30_INSTALL_DIR: ./configure.sh --with-mt build/release_mt build clasp multithread support custom directory structure: ./configure.sh --with-mt TBB_INCLUDE=<path_to_tbb_include> TBB_LIB=<path_to_tbb_lib> build/release_mt install clasp: install default, 'make install' install clasp '/usr/local/bin' installation prefix '/usr/local' running configure-script option '--prefix=PATH'. Alternatively, option '--bindir=PATH' installation path. Finally, skip installation simply copy clasp executable directory choice. BUILDING Microsoft Visual Studio directory build_vc/ provide Microsoft Visual Studio project files building clasp. download freely express edition Visual http://www.microsoft.com/express/Downloads/ installed: open build_vc\vc9\clasp\clasp.sln select desired solution configuration (typically release_static) build "app" project USAGE clasp reads problem instances stdin, cat problem clasp file, clasp problem logic programs SMODELS-format, clasp supports SAT-problems DIMACS-, Max-SAT (extended) DIMACS-, PB-problems OPB WBO-format. Type clasp overview options supported clasp. addition printing status information, clasp information computation exit status. exit status problem satisfiable problem proved unsatisfiable satisfiability problem search interrupted started clasp ran memory exit status error.

This is the hmi_robin meta-package

The speech_database package performs text-to-speech conversion by using Google's speech synthesiser. 
	  It also provides a storage solution for synthesised speech.
	  Speech can be accessed by publishing a text string at the /speech topic. If no entry exists, the text will by synthesised and stored locally in an mp3 file. 
	  After an entry that corresponds to the text was found or created, a request to play the audio file is published at the /robotsound topic to be processed by the soundplay_node.py of the sound_play package.

hrl_kdl contains wrappers for interfacing with KDL and other utilities
        for dealing with kinematics.

pykdl_utils contains kdl_parser.py, for parsing URDF objects
     from the robot_model_py stack into PyKDL trees and chains, 
     and kdl_kinematics.py, for wrapping KDL kinematics calls, making
     kinematics requests in Python far simpler.  jointspace_kdl_kin.py
     also contains a KDLKinematics superclass which subscribes to /joint_states,
     automatically filling the FK and jacobian requests with the current joint
     angles.
A small kinematics library for humanoid robots based on KDL. The main components are (recursive) center of mass, (static) stability, and support polygon computations.

readme: softhand_ros ROS package SoftHand Note package depends branch pazeshun/dynamixel_motor@gripper-v6-devel Citation Softhand @INPROCEEDINGS{hirose_softhand_v1, author={Hirose, Toshinori Kakiuchi, Yohei Okada, Kei Inaba, Masayuki}, booktitle={2019 IEEE/RSJ International Conference Intelligent Robots Systems (IROS)}, title={Design Soft Flexible Wire-driven Finger Mechanism Contact Pressure Distribution}, year={2019}, volume={}, number={}, pages={4699-4705}, doi={10.1109/IROS40897.2019.8968011} Softhand @INPROCEEDINGS{hirose_softhand_v2, author={Hirose, Toshinori Kitagawa, Shingo Hasegawa, Shun Kakiuchi, Yohei Okada, Kei Inaba, Masayuki}, booktitle={2022 IEEE International Conference Soft Robotics (RoboSoft)}, title={Waterproof Soft Robot Hand Variable Stiffness Wire-driven Finger Mechanism Melting Point Alloy Contact Pressure Distribution Concentration}, year={2022}, volume={}, number={}, pages={109-116}, doi={10.1109/RoboSoft54090.2022.9762208} Installation Workspace build source /opt/ros/$ROS_DISTRO/setup.bash mkdir ~/softhand_ws/src ~/softhand_ws/src wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/knorth55/softhand_ros/master/fc.rosinstall .rosinstall wstool rosdep install --ignore-src --from-paths ~/softhand_ws catkin build Udev installation source ~/softhand_ws/devel/setup.bash roscd softhand_ros sudo udev/*.rules /etc/udev/rules.d sudo service udev reload sudo service udev restart Softhand Description Softhand Softhand designed contact pressure distribution. hand grasp soft fragile object power, solid heavy object high power. detailed information, read Design Soft Flexible Wire-driven Finger Mechanism Contact Pressure Distribution Softhand Softhand designed waterproof variable stiffness. hand grasp softhand massage human’s head hair. detailed information read Waterproof Soft Robot Hand Variable Stiffness Wire-driven Finger Mechanism Melting Point Alloy Contact Pressure Distribution Concentration. description: jsk_kinova_startup
The kinovaeus package
jsk_magni_startup
readme: jsk_magnistartup SetUp (Running commands time robot) fix ROS_HOSTNAME robot Edit /etc/ubiquity/env.sh #!/bin/sh #export ROS_HOSTNAME=$(hostname).local export ROS_HOSTNAME=$(hostname) export ROS_MASTER_URI=http://$ROS_HOSTNAME:11311 Setup pigpid.services https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/pi_sonar, wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/joan2937/pigpio/master/util/pigpiod.service sudo pigpiod.service /etc/systemd/system sudo systemctl enable pigpiod.service sudo systemctl start pigpiod.service add jsk_magni.service Add /etc/systemd/system/jsk-magni-startup.service file [Unit] Requires=roscore.service PartOf=roscore.service After=magni-base.service [Service] Type=simple User=ubuntu ExecStart=/usr/sbin/jsk-magni-startup [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target Add /usr/sbin/jsk-magni-startup #!/bin/bash function log() logger user.$1 log info "magni-base: workspace setup file /home/ubuntu/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash" source /home/ubuntu/catkin_ws/devel/setup.bash log_path="/tmp" source /etc/ubiquity/env.sh log info "magni-base: Launching ROS_HOSTNAME=$ROS_HOSTNAME, ROS_IP=$ROS_IP, ROS_MASTER_URI=$ROS_MASTER_URI, ROS_LOG_DIR=$log_path" Punch export ROS_HOME=$(echo ~ubuntu)/.ros export ROS_LOG_DIR=$log_path roslaunch jsk_magni_startup magni_bringup.launch PID=$! log info "jsk-magni-startup: Started roslaunch background process, PID $PID, ROS_LOG_DIR=$ROS_LOG_DIR" echo "$PID" $log_path/jsk-magni-startup.pid wait "$PID" Enable service sudo systemctl enable jsk-magni-startup.service sudo systemctl start jsk-magni-startup.service Add latest codes catkin_ws Update catkin_ws/src git: local-name: demos uri: https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/demos.git version: 52136f04c0f39fe6a1001a17e208e5ce5b4cda61 git: local-name: magni_robot uri: https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/magni_robot version: 603cae184bcc59b96d59f2fee6029b22725c3c5c git: local-name: pi_sonar uri: https://github.com/UbiquityRobotics/pi_sonar version: b84458f909d5febd410b8cd68d552e9aecad685c

readme: JSK EusLisp repository repository containts software devleoped JSK University Tokyo. started Install binary (RECOMMENDED: Ubuntu Setup ROS repository sudo 'echo "deb http://packages.ros.org/ros/ubuntu `lsb_release main" /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros-latest.list' wget http://packages.ros.org/ros.key sudo apt-key add echo "source /opt/ros/indigo/setup.bash" ~/.bashrc sudo apt-get update Install Euslisp sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-euslisp Install Homebrew (OSX) brew install euslisp/jskeus/jskeus Playing Demo programs Motion generation irteusgl irteus/demo/demo.l type command, ‘(crank-motion)’ ‘(dual-arm-ik)’ Robots objects models irteusgl models/irt-all-robots.l "(make-all-robots)" irteusgl models/irt-all-objects.l "(make-all-objects)" started ROS users Install binaries (RECOMMENDED: Ubuntu Setup ROS repository skip procdeure setup ROS system sudo 'echo "deb http://packages.ros.org/ros/ubuntu `lsb_release main" /etc/apt/sources.list.d/ros-latest.list' wget http://packages.ros.org/ros.key sudo apt-key add echo "source /opt/ros/indigo/setup.bash" ~/.bashrc sudo apt-get update Install pr2/euslisp programs sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-pr2eus Running demo programs Visualizinot robot rosrun euscollada pr2.sh real users set ROS_MASTER_URI ROS_IP robot simulated command roscd pr2eus read joint state display euslisp viewer, print jacobian roslaunch ./pr2-read-state.launch sample program show posture euslisp, MOVE REAL ROBOT, CAUTION!!! roslaunch ./pr2-send-joints.launch script set ROS_MASTER_URI ROS_IP source `rospack find jsk_tools`/src/bashrc.ros rossetrobot pr1012.jsk.com change environment rossetip roseus package http://github.com/jsk-ros-pkg repository ROS client library euslisp Support Trouble Shooting issue tracker support pull requests report bugs patches. Online Documents Japanese avilable) online manual PDF files Install euslisp source code (EXPERTS Installing dependent libraries Ubuntu users sudo apt-get install git gcc libjpeg-dev libxext-dev libx11-dev libgl1-mesa-dev libglu1-mesa-dev libpq-dev libpng12-dev xfonts-100dpi xfonts-75dpi gsfonts-x11 texlive-fonts-extra xfonts-100dpi-transcoded xfonts-75dpi-transcoded msttcorefonts Mac OSX users Homebrew brew install jpeg libpng mesalib-glw wget ’'’NOTE:’’’ EusLisp Library, Mac OSX mountain lion longer support install substitution XQuartz. Mac OSX lion older) users install latest XQuartz safety. XQuartz older installation EusLisp fail. install latest XQuartz.) Raspberry users debian(Raspbian “wheezy”) sudo apt-get install git gcc libjpeg62-dev libxext-dev libx11-dev libgl1-mesa-dev libglu1-mesa-dev libpq-dev libpng12-dev xfonts-100dpi xfonts-75dpi additional install OpenGL raspberry sudo apt-get install libgl1-mesa-dri xserver-xorg-video-vesa xutils Cygwin(now working 32bit Packages listed intalled setup.exe. git, gcc-core, gcc-g++, libjpeg-devel, libpng-devel libGL-devel, libGLU-devel, libglut-devel, libX11-devel, libXext-devel xorg-server X-start-menu-icons startxwin.exe) mesa-demo glxinfo) bitmap fonts bitmap font packages -75dpi, -100dpi, -misc Downloading building EusLisp git clone http://github.com/euslisp/jskeus jskeus jskeus instructions add EUSDIR, ARCHDIR, PATH, LD_LIBRARY_PATH environment variable bashrc invoke euslisp program echo "source ~/bashrc.eus" ~/.bashrc Install ROS jsk-ros-pkg repository http://wiki.ros.org/indigo/Installation/Source detail sudo apt-get install python-rosdep python-rosinstall-generator python-wstool python-rosinstall build-essential mkdir ~/ros_catkin_ws/src ~/ros_catkin_ws/src wstool init src https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_pr2eus/master/.rosinstall wget https://raw.githubusercontent.com/jsk-ros-pkg/jsk_travis/master/rosdep-install.sh ./rosdep-install.sh catkin_make source devel/setup.bash Acknowledgment software repository based EusLisp language.

description: Wraps the kdl and kdl_parser packages for generating KDL kinematic chains from URDF.
readme: kdl_acc_solver Overview Recursive KDL solver joint space acceleration.

description: C++ wrapper for easily using KDL kinematic solvers with robots defined in ROS through URDF files. Wraps the kdl and kdl_parser packages for generating KDL kinematic chains from URDF by just taking as inputs the IDs of the root and tip of the kinematic chain of a robot manipulator.
readme: kdl_wrapper Overview wrapper easily KDL kinematic solvers robots defined ROS URDF files. Wraps kdl kdl_parser packages generating KDL kinematic chains URDF inputs IDs root tip kinematic chain robot manipulator. initializes KDL solvers: Inverse velocity solver (WDLS) joint jacobian solver joint pose solver joint acceleration solver Requires kdl_acc_solver package installed catkin workspace: git clone https://github.com/kth-ros-pkg/kdl_acc_solver.git code run code src/pr2_kdl_wrapper_example.cpp pr2_common metapackage: sudo apt-get install ros-<rosdistro>-pr2-common Upload URDF: roslaunch pr2_description upload_pr2.launch kinect:=TRUE Compile kdl_wrapper package. Run pr2_kdl_wrapper_example rosrun kdl_wrapper pr2_kdl_wrapper_example

libccd is library for collision detection between two convex shapes.
readme: libccd libccd library collision detection convex shapes. libccd implements variation Gilbert–Johnson–Keerthi algorithm Expand Polytope Algorithm (EPA) implements algorithm Minkowski Portal Refinement (MPR, a.k.a. XenoCollide) Game Programming Gems libccd open source library knowledge include MPR algorithm working space. library called mpr2d, implemented programming language, works space. libccd part ODE library ODE’s ./configure enable FCL library Willow Garage, Bullet3 library (https://github.com/bulletphysics/bullet3). implementation details GJK algorithm, http://www.win.tue.nl/~gino/solid/jgt98convex.pdf. Dependencies library based standard libraries. exception testsuites built top (https://github.com/danfis/cu) library licensed LGPL, testing depends libccd library distributed License libccd licensed OSI-approved 3-clause BSD License, text license distributed source code BSD-LICENSE file. file include license notice, rest considered licensed 3-clause BSD License. Compile Install libccd mechanisms compiling installing. simple Makefile, autotools, CMake. Makefile Directory src/ Makefile needed compilation installation: src/ install Library libccd default compiled double precision floating point numbers change options USE_SINGLE/USE_DOUBLE, USE_SINGLE=yes compile library single precision. Installation directory changed options PREFIX, INCLUDEDIR LIBDIR. info type Autotools libccd support autotools: Generate configure script ./bootstrap Create build/ directory: mkdir build build Run configure script: ../configure Run install: install configure script change libccd compiled installed, significant option –enable-double-precision enables double precision (single default case). CMake build mkdir build build cmake "Unix Makefiles" install build ninja: mkdir build build cmake Ninja ninja ninja install build tools generator. cmake Xcode cmake "Visual Studio compile double precision, set ENABLE_DOUBLE_PRECISION option: mkdir build build cmake "Unix Makefiles" -DENABLE_DOUBLE_PRECISION=ON install build libccd shared library, set BUILD_SHARED_LIBS option: mkdir build build cmake "Unix Makefiles" -DBUILD_SHARED_LIBS=ON install build test suite, set BUILD_TESTING option: mkdir build build cmake "Unix Makefiles" -DBUILD_TESTING=ON test installation directory changed CMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX variable: mkdir build build cmake "Unix Makefiles" -DCMAKE_INSTALL_PREFIX=/path/to/install install GJK Intersection Test section describes libccd testing convex objects intersects ‘yes/no’ test) Gilbert-Johnson-Keerthi (GJK) algorithm. Procedure simple similar usages library): Include <ccd/ccd.h> file. Implement support function specific shapes. Support function function returns furthest point object (shape) direction. Set ccd_t structure. Run ccdGJKIntersect() function desired objects. skeleton simple program: #include <ccd/ccd.h> #include <ccd/quat.h> work quaternions Support function box void support(const void *obj, const ccd_vec3_t *dir, ccd_vec3_t *vec) assume obj_t user-defined structure holds info object case box: pos, quat dimensions box, position rotation) obj_t *obj (obj_t *)_obj; ccd_vec3_t dir; ccd_quat_t qinv; apply rotation direction vector ccdVec3Copy(&dir, _dir); ccdQuatInvert2(&qinv, &obj->quat); ccdQuatRotVec(&dir, &qinv); compute support point direction ccdVec3Set(v, ccdSign(ccdVec3X(&dir)) box->x CCD_REAL(0.5), ccdSign(ccdVec3Y(&dir)) box->y CCD_REAL(0.5), ccdSign(ccdVec3Z(&dir)) box->z CCD_REAL(0.5)); transform support point position rotation object ccdQuatRotVec(v, &obj->quat); ccdVec3Add(v, &obj->pos); int main(int argc, char *argv[]) ccd_t ccd; CCD_INIT(&ccd); initialize ccd_t struct set ccd_t struct ccd.support1 support; support function object ccd.support2 support; support function object ccd.max_iterations maximal number iterations int intersect ccdGJKIntersect(obj1, obj2, &ccd); intersect holds true obj1 obj2 intersect, false GJK EPA Penetration Objects obtain penetration info intersection objects ccdGJKPenetration() function Procedure previous case: #include <ccd/ccd.h> #include <ccd/quat.h> work quaternions Support function previous case int main(int argc, char *argv[]) ccd_t ccd; CCD_INIT(&ccd); initialize ccd_t struct set ccd_t struct ccd.support1 support; support function object ccd.support2 support; support function object ccd.max_iterations maximal number iterations ccd.epa_tolerance maximal tolerance fro EPA part ccd_real_t depth; ccd_vec3_t dir, pos; int intersect ccdGJKPenetration(obj1, obj2, &ccd, &depth, &dir, &pos); intersect holds obj1 obj2 intersect, depth, dir pos stored penetration depth, direction separation vector position global coordinate system MPR Intersection Test libccd MPR Minkowski Portal Refinement algorithm testing objects intersects. Procedure similar GJK algorithm. Support function function returns center point center) object implemented: #include <ccd/ccd.h> #include <ccd/quat.h> work quaternions Support function previous case Center function returns center object void center(const void *_obj, ccd_vec3_t *center) obj_t *obj (obj_t *)_obj; ccdVec3Copy(center, &obj->pos); int main(int argc, char *argv[]) ccd_t ccd; CCD_INIT(&ccd); initialize ccd_t struct set ccd_t struct ccd.support1 support; support function object ccd.support2 support; support function object ccd.center1 center; center function object ccd.center2 center; center function object ccd.mpr_tolerance maximal tolerance int intersect ccdMPRIntersect(obj1, obj2, &ccd); intersect holds true obj1 obj2 intersect, false MPR Penetration Objects MPR algorithm obtaining penetration info intersection objects equally easy previous case ccdMPRPenetration() function #include <ccd/ccd.h> #include <ccd/quat.h> work quaternions Support function previous case Center function prevous case int main(int argc, char *argv[]) ccd_t ccd; CCD_INIT(&ccd); initialize ccd_t struct set ccd_t struct ccd.support1 support; support function object ccd.support2 support; support function object ccd.center1 center; center function object ccd.center2 center; center function object ccd.mpr_tolerance maximal tolerance ccd_real_t depth; ccd_vec3_t dir, pos; int intersect ccdMPRPenetration(obj1, obj2, &ccd, &depth, &dir, &pos); intersect holds obj1 obj2 intersect, depth, dir pos stored penetration depth, direction separation vector position global coordinate system

description:
A basic grasp generator for simple objects such as blocks or cylinders for use with the MoveIt! pick and place pipeline. 
    Does not consider friction cones or other dynamics.
readme: MoveIt! Simple Grasps basic grasp generator simple objects blocks cylinders MoveIt! pick place pipeline. friction cones dynamics. current implementation simple takes input pose vector (postition orientation) generates large number potential grasp approaches directions. includes grasp filter removing kinematically infeasible grasps threaded solvers. package includes: Simple pose-based grasp generator block Separate grasp generators custom objects rectanguar cylindrical objects Grasp filter Test code visualizations Developed Dave Coleman Correll Robotics Lab, University Colorado Boulder contributors. Video Demo simple demo Baxter: Build Status Install Ubuntu Debian Hydro: sudo apt-get install ros-hydro-moveit-simple-grasps Indigo: sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-moveit-simple-grasps Install Source Clone repository catkin workspace, rosdep install tool automatically download dependencies. Depending current version ROS, Hydro: rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro hydro Indigo: rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro indigo Robot-Agnostic Configuration configuration file robot’s effector geometry. format repository config/baxter_grasp_data.yaml. comments file explanations. load file launch, copy file launch/grasp_test.launch line <rosparam command="load" file="$(find moveit_simple_grasps)/config/baxter_grasp_data.yaml"/>. Code Usage Note: find moveit_blocks.h discussed bottom page, helpful. discuss generation, filtering, visualization components. robot’s ROS package, add package package.xml, CMakeLists.txt. file add includes: Grasp generation visualization #include <moveit_simple_grasps/simple_grasps.h> #include <moveit_simple_grasps/grasp_data.h> #include <moveit_visual_tools/visual_tools.h> Add class’s member variables Grasp generator moveit_simple_grasps::SimpleGraspsPtr simple_grasps_; class publishing stuff rviz moveit_visual_tools::VisualToolsPtr visual_tools_; robot-specific data generating grasps moveit_simple_grasps::GraspData grasp_data_; class’ constructor initialize visualization tools; Load Robot Tools publishing Rviz visual_tools_.reset(new moveit_visual_tools::VisualTools("base_link")); Change parameter visual tools robot’s base link. information package, moveit_visual_tools. load robot’s custom .yaml grasp data file: Load grasp data specific robot ros::NodeHandle (!grasp_data_.loadRobotGraspData(nh, "left_hand")) ros::shutdown(); “left_hand” SRDF-defined MoveIt! effectors Setup Assistant. data loaded file load parameter server roslaunch file, desribed load grasp generator: Load grasp generator simple_grasps_.reset( moveit_simple_grasps::SimpleGrasps(visual_tools_) generate grasps, pose object grasp, block. Here’s pose: geometry_msgs::Pose object_pose; object_pose.position.x object_pose.position.y object_pose.position.z Orientation double angle M_PI Eigen::Quaterniond quat(Eigen::AngleAxis<double>(double(angle), Eigen::Vector3d::UnitZ())); object_pose.orientation.x quat.x(); object_pose.orientation.y quat.y(); object_pose.orientation.z quat.z(); object_pose.orientation.w quat.w(); visualize object pose block: visual_tools_->publishBlock(object_pose, moveit_visual_tools::BLUE, generate grasps: std::vector<moveit_msgs::Grasp> possible_grasps; simple_grasps_->generateBlockGrasps( object_pose, grasp_data_, possible_grasps); visualize: visual_tools_->publishAnimatedGrasps(possible_grasps, grasp_data_.ee_parent_link_); Grasp Filter Usage component creates threads tests large number potential grasps kinematic feasibility. filter grasps generating Filter grasp reachable bool filter_pregrasps true; std::vector<trajectory_msgs::JointTrajectoryPoint> ik_solutions; save grasps solution visualization grasp_filter_->filterGrasps(possible_grasps, ik_solutions, filter_pregrasps, grasp_data_.ee_parent_link_, planning_group_name_); view filtered grasps planning group pose: visual_tools_->publishIKSolutions(ik_solutions, planning_group_name_, undocumented tired writing Tested Robots Baxter REEM Code development) tool moveit_blocks.h located include folder. complete pick place pipeline package MoveIt, config file launch file. launch file implementations: baxter_pick_place reem_tabletop_grasping clam_pick_place Testing tests scripts package. view tests, start Rviz roslaunch moveit_simple_grasps grasp_test_rviz.launch test grasp generation randomly blocks: roslaunch moveit_simple_grasps grasp_test.launch test grasp filtering: roslaunch moveit_simple_grasps grasp_filter_test.launch Contributors Dave Coleman, Boulder @davetcoleman Bence Magyar, PAL Robotics @bmagyar

description: Helper functions for displaying and debugging MoveIt! data in Rviz via published markers
readme: MoveIt! Visual Tools Helper functions displaying debugging MoveIt! data Rviz published markers, trajectories, MoveIt! collision objects. hard understand internally MoveIt!, quick convenience functions easily visualize code. package includes: Basic geometric markers Rviz MoveIt! collision object tools Trajectory visualization tools Robot state tools Developed Dave Coleman Correll Robotics Lab, University Colorado Boulder contributors. Build Status Install Ubuntu Debian sudo apt-get install ros-hydro-moveit-visual-tools sudo apt-get install ros-indigo-moveit-visual-tools Install Source Clone repository catkin workspace, rosdep install tool automatically download dependencies. Depending current version ROS, rosdep install --from-paths src --ignore-src --rosdistro indigo Usage assume helper functions class. Initialize Add includes: #include <moveit_visual_tools/visual_tools.h> Add class’s member variables: visualizing rviz moveit_visual_tools::VisualToolsPtr visual_tools_; class’ constructor add: visual_tools_.reset(new moveit_visual_tools::VisualTools("base_frame","/moveit_visual_markers")); Change parameter robot’s base frame, parameter you’d Rviz marker ROS topic. settings adjust, documenting future: visual_tools_->setMuted(false); visual_tools_->setLifetime(20.0); visual_tools_->setEEGroupName(grasp_data_.ee_group_); visual_tools_->setPlanningGroupName(planning_group_name_); visual_tools_->setFloorToBaseHeight(floor_to_base_height); visual_tools_->setGraspPoseToEEFPose(grasp_pose_to_eef_pose); visual_tools_->setAlpha(alpha); visual_tools_->setGlobalScale(scale); visual_tools_->setBaseFrame(frame_name); Tools code easily debug MoveIt! code visual markers Rviz Start rviz create marker ‘Add’ button bottom Choose marker topic topic constructor. Code snippet create pose xyz rotate pose degrees axis. publish pose arrow visualziation Rviz. Rviz fixed frame chosen code. description:
razor_imu_9dof is a package that provides a ROS driver
     for the Sparkfun Razor IMU 9DOF. It also provides Arduino
     firmware that runs on the Razor board, and which must be
     installed on the Razor board for the system to work. A node
     which displays the attitude (roll, pitch and yaw) of the Razor board 
     (or any IMU) is provided for testing.
authors: Tang Tiong Yew, Kristof Robot, Paul Bouchier, Peter Bartz
readme: Official ROS Documentation extensive standard ROS-style version documentation ROS wiki http://wiki.ros.org/razor_imu_9dof Install Configure ROS Package Install dependencies: sudo apt-get install python-visual Download code: ~/catkin_workspace/src git clone https://github.com/KristofRobot/razor_imu_9dof.git catkin_make Install Arduino firmware Open src/Razor_AHRS/Razor_AHRS.ino Arduino IDE. Note: modified version Peter Bartz’ original Arduino code https://github.com/ptrbrtz/razor-9dof-ahrs). version emits linear acceleration angular velocity data required ROS Imu message Select hardware uncommenting line src/Razor_AHRS/Razor_AHRS.ino, HARDWARE OPTIONS Select hardware uncommenting line! //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Razor IMU" version "SEN-10125" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Razor IMU" version "SEN-10736" (HMC5883L magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10183" (HMC5843 magnetometer) //#define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10321" (HMC5843 magnetometer) #define HW__VERSION_CODE SparkFun "9DOF Sensor Stick" version "SEN-10724" (HMC5883L magnetometer) Upload Arduino sketch Sparkfun 9DOF Razor IMU board Configure default configuration, razor_imu_9dof expects yaml config file my_razor.yaml USB port Calibration parameters examplerazor.yaml file Copy file my_razor.yaml roscd razor_imu_9dof/config razor.yaml my_razor.yaml edit my_razor.yaml needed Launch Publisher visualization: roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub-and-display.launch Publisher roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub.launch Publisher diagnostics: roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-pub-diags.launch visualization roslaunch razor_imu_9dof razor-display.launch Calibrate accuracy, follow tutorial calibrate sensors: http://wiki.ros.org/razor_imu_9dof copy Peter Bartz’s magnetometer calibration scripts https://github.com/ptrbrtz/razor-9dof-ahrs magnetometer_calibration directory. Update my_razor.yaml calibration parameters. Dynamic Reconfigure launched publisher launch commands listed dynamically reconfigure yaw calibration. Run: rosrun rqt_reconfigure rqt_reconfigure Select imu_node. Change slider move calibration degrees. running visualization you’ll display jump calibration takes effect. intent feature tune alignment AHRS direction robot driving direction, determine AHRS reads degrees robot degrees shown GPS, tune calibration read compass-equivalent bore-sighting camera.

Sample Source rosruby sample programs rosruby_tutorials package. Subscriber #!/usr/bin/env ruby require 'ros' require 'std_msgs/String' node ROS::Node.new('/rosruby_sample_subscriber') node.subscribe('/chatter', Std_msgs::String) |msg| puts "message \'#{msg.data}\'" node.ok? node.spin_once sleep(1) Publisher #!/usr/bin/env ruby require 'ros' require 'std_msgs/String' node ROS::Node.new('/rosruby_sample_publisher') publisher node.advertise('/chatter', Std_msgs::String) msg Std_msgs::String.new node.ok? msg.data rosruby!: publisher.publish(msg) sleep(1.0) Note Ruby requires ‘Start Capital letter’ class module names. Std_msgs::String class std_msgs::String. rosruby message compiler automatically generates messages rule. description:
The rviz_fps_plugin package contains an additional ViewController and a Tool Plugin to navigate RViz like an FPS-Shooter.

    To enable the tool press 'q' and enable the 'FPSMotion' ViewController. Use the 'wasd' keys for walking.
    By pressing 'f' one can switch between walk or fly mode. By pressing 'r' you can reset the view controller.
readme: rviz_fps_plugin rviz_fps_plugin package additional ViewController Tool Plugin navigate RViz FPS-Shooter. enable tool press enable ‘FPSMotion’ ViewController. ‘wasd’ keys walking. pressing switch walk fly mode. pressing reset view controller.

High-level applications that run on Segway RMP 50 based robots at Learning
    Agents Research Group (LARG), AI Laboratory, Department of Computer
    Science, The University of Texas at Austin.

Displays a simple GUI to the user. The GUI is capable of displaying a simple
    message or image to the user, and ask a question. The message and question
    plugins are also written in this package.

High-level navigation application for the segbot allowing the segbot to
    approach and gothrough doors. The application can also be used to determine
    the segbot's logical location, as well as sense when a door in front of the
    robot is open or not.

Contains launch files for running the ROS navigation stack on the segbot
    using the eband_local_planner approach, as well as launch files for amcl
    and gmapping.

Wraps a Human-Robot Interaction Experiment conducted at the
    University of Texas At Austin to use the Robotics in Concert Framework. The
    results from this experiment were reported in the following symposium
    paper: Piyush Khandelwal and Peter Stone. Multi-robot Human Guidance using
    Topological Graphs. In AAAI Spring 2014 Symposium on Qualitative
    Representations for Robots (AAAI-SSS), March 2014.

    same pattern as turtle_concert.

The rmp_base package provides a ros interface to control a Segway Robotics Mobility Platform. 
    In addition, navigation and status data are also published such as odometry, imu, mottor and battery status, ...
The rmp_description package provides Unified Robot Description Format files to represent a Segway Robotics Mobility Platform.
The rmp_msgs package defines rmp specific messages such as motor status, audio command, ...

The rmp_teleop package provides teleoperation functionalities for a Segway Robotics Mobility Platform.
    This package currently supports the xbox wireless joytsick.

description:
This stack regroups the different ros interfaces developped for Shadow Robot's
  Hardware. It provides an interface to both simulated and real hardware.
sr_description contains the description for Shadow Robot's Hand and Arm, as well as some additional models used in our robot (kinect, etc...).
sr_example is an example of a real package interfaced with our robot. Please refer to the tutorial Creating a package to interact with our robots.
Gazebo Plugins for various Shadow Robot-specific sensors and actuators on the robot.
authors: adapted by Ugo(software <AT> shadowrobot.com), Sachin Chitta, Stu Glaser, John Hsu

description:
This is a ROS interface to the Shadow Robot's robotic hand. It
     contains both an interface to the real hand (communicating via
     a CAN interface) and a simulated version of the hand. It also
     contains an interface to Shadow Robot's muscle arm.
This package contains the actuator used in the hand model.

A specific shadowhand package derived from arm_kinematics, for computing both forward and backward kinematics for the fingers except thumb.
    Solution is analytic. Developed as an alternative to arm_kinematics that cannot solve coupled joints.

This library offers an introspection mechanism for C/C++ value-types. I.e.
      it offers a way to represent types, and to manipulate in-memory values
      that are instances of those types.

      A Ruby binding is included, which gives a fast and transparent
      modification of C/C++ in-memory types from Ruby, and an associated
      interface to call C functions from shared libraries.

description: The template package

The v4r_artoolkitplus package is a wrapper around the ARToolKitPlus software library for ROS.
  The ARToolKitPlus is a extended version of ARToolKit's library with a class-based API but with no VRML and camera library.
  It is also optimized to work with a set of precoded marker, so no marker training is needed.
  The ARToolKitPlus library was published under GPL2, thanks for that work goes to all people listed in a ./3rdParty/ARToolKitPlus/THANKS.
  More details about ARToolKitPlus package can be found under http://studierstube.org/handheld_ar/artoolkitplus.php

The v4r_ellipses package contains a computer vision library which is able to detect ellipses within images.  
    The package is able to estimate the pose of the circle related to the ellipse the circle diameter as well as the camera parameter are known.
    A dynamic reconfigure interface allows the user to tune the parameter of the system to ones needs.
    But be aware that the pose of a projected circle within a image (ellipse) has two solutions and only one is published as TF.
    A costom message (v4r_msgs) publishes all the data as well as the pose ambiguity.

The v4r_laser_filter package filters sensor_msgs::LaserScan messages.
  Currently there are two filters, max range, and a line filter. 
  The max range filter removes inf values and replaces them with max range. 
  This comes handy by clearing cost maps.

The v4r_opencv_cam package is a camera driver package which

  cameras.

The contact_handler package. It reports the dynamic-engine-reported contacts between
      all scene objects on a topic called "contacts".

      This package uses gazebo_msgs/ContactsState message type to report body contacts.
      This is because ROS doesn't have any standardized contact reporting messages.
      However, to have gazebo_msgs, you don't need to install whole Gazebo, you just need to
      install the single package ros-indigo-gazebo-msgs.

This Package contains a ROS-brige for the VRMagic Smartcameras. There is a
	ROS-node (vrmagic_ros_bridge_server), which is the Server for the ROS-brige,
  	and firmware for the VRMagic Smartcam. On the Smartcam the User have to implement a firmware and have	
	to use the Class VrMagicHandler_camhost for communication with the vrmagic_ros_bridge_server.
	Each Image which is transmitted to the vrmagic_ros_bridge_server by using the Class VrMagicHandler_camhost
  	will be published as Image via ROS. Multiple Images are also possible.

description:
Installer script for the mingw cross environment. This will install to /opt/mingw
     and immediately begin cross-compiling gcc, boost and qt. Other libraries can be added by
     simply cd'ing to /opt/mingw and running make for the desired target.

Windows python scripts aren't recognised as executables, so alot of the ros
    build logic will fail when trying to call python scripts because they 
    utilise the bash logic at the top of the script.
     
    This remedies the problem by creating a batch script for each that calls
    the relevant python script.
    
    It also provides various other scripts, utilities, launchers and dependency
    targets for setting up the runtime.
readme: #section Overview Windows python scripts aren’t recognised executables, alot ros build logic fail call python scripts utilise bash logic top script. remedies problem wrapping build/bin python script exe’s batch files. #section Fugly problems standardised modules py2exe pyInstaller working ros. quickly hacked self-describing executable job. Installation simply copies exe build/bin. Batch scripts alot simpler, throw annoying prompt ctrl-c conformation termination. set roslaunch scripts shell running subprocess.

description: Supporting tools and demos for developing with ros on windows.

XDot.py, by Jose Fonseca, is an interactive viewer for graphs written in
    Graphviz's dot language.

    This package adds front-end capabilities to XDot including WX Widget
    support and experimental Qt support and a mechanism for receiving callbacks
    when nodes are clicked.  This extension is provided as BSD.
authors: Jose Fonseca, Jonathan Bohren, Alex Bravo
readme: xdot XDot.py, Jose Fonseca, interactive viewer graphs written Graphviz’s dot language. package adds front-end capabilities XDot including Widget support experimental support mechanism receiving callbacks nodes clicked. extension BSD.
